concepts.description: The largest artery in the human body, originating from the left ventricle and distributing oxygenated blood to systemic circulation via its ascending, arch, and descending segments
Blood vessels supplying and draining the heart muscle, comprising the coronary arteries and cardiac veins that perfuse the myocardium
Organ system consisting of the heart, blood vessels, and blood that functions to circulate oxygen, nutrients, hormones, and waste products throughout the body
Venous vessels of the head that drain deoxygenated blood from the brain, meninges, skull, and facial structures, returning it to the internal jugular and vertebral veins
A hollow muscular organ of the mediastinum that functions as a dual pump, circulating blood through pulmonary and systemic circuits via coordinated rhythmic contractions
concepts.shape: Roughly conical, fist-sized organ with a broad base superiorly, a blunt apex directed inferolaterally, and sternocostal, diaphragmatic, and pulmonary surfaces
concepts.composition: cardiac muscle, fibrous connective tissue, endothelium, epicardium, fibrous pericardium, serous pericardium, smooth muscle, elastic tissue, adipose tissue

structure.text: Cardiac valves (tricuspid, mitral, pulmonary, and aortic)

nerves.text: Superior cervical cardiac branch of vagus nerve
Inferior cervical cardiac branch of vagus nerve
Thoracic cardiac branch of vagus nerve

concepts.description: A large venous vessel that carries nutrient-rich, deoxygenated blood from the gastrointestinal tract and spleen to the liver for processing and detoxification
Pulmonary artery arising from the bifurcation of the main pulmonary trunk that carries deoxygenated blood from the right ventricle to the left lung for gas exchange
Blood vessels comprising the pulmonary arteries and veins that circulate deoxygenated blood from the right heart to the lungs and return oxygenated blood to the left heart
Proximal segment of the great vessel arising from the right ventricle, anchored at the conus arteriosus and encircled by the aortic root at its fibrous base
Large venous trunk that drains deoxygenated blood from the head, neck, upper limbs, and thorax into the right atrium of the heart
Vessels of the systemic circulation that carry oxygenated blood from the left heart to body tissues, distributing nutrients and oxygen throughout all organs except the lungs
Vessels of the venous circulation that return deoxygenated blood from the body's tissues to the right atrium, excluding the pulmonary veins
Paired dural venous sinuses running horizontally along the occipital bone's posterior surface, draining the confluence of sinuses laterally toward the sigmoid sinuses and jugular foramina
Fibrous and valvular apparatus of the heart comprising the annuli, leaflets, chordae, and papillary muscles that collectively regulate unidirectional blood flow through cardiac chambers
Synovial hinge joint formed by the tibia, fibula, and talus that permits dorsiflexion and plantarflexion of the foot
Joints united by cartilage, permitting limited or no movement, classified as synchondroses when hyaline cartilage is present or symphyses when fibrocartilage connects opposing bony surfaces
Synchondroses and symphyses connecting the ribs, sternum, and costal cartilages, providing flexible yet stable articulations that permit thoracic expansion during respiration
Fibrocartilaginous and hyaline cartilage unions between vertebral bodies and neurocentral elements that permit limited motion while providing axial load distribution and spinal flexibility
Synovial plane joints between the heads of ribs and the costal facets of thoracic vertebral bodies, permitting gliding motion during respiration
Immovable fibrous articulations of the skull where adjacent cranial bones are tightly bound by dense connective tissue, forming rigid sutures that protect the brain
A freely movable synovial joint characterized by a joint cavity, articular cartilage, and a fibrous capsule lined with synovial membrane, permitting a wide range of motion
A synovial hinge joint formed by the articulation of the humerus, radius, and ulna, enabling flexion and extension of the forearm with contributing pronation and supination
Joints united by dense fibrous connective tissue with no synovial cavity, permitting little to no movement, classified as sutures, syndesmoses, or gomphoses

concepts.canonicalName: Fibrous joints of free part of lower limb
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Fibrous joints of free part of lower limb
sourceObjectNames: Fibrous joints of free part of lower limb.g
concepts.description: Immovable or slightly mobile articulations in the lower limb's free segment where bones are united by dense fibrous connective tissue, including syndesmoses and sutures, allowing minimal to no movement

concepts.canonicalName: Fibrous joints of free upper limb
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Fibrous joints of free upper limb
sourceObjectNames: Fibrous joints of free upper limb.g
concepts.description: Immovable or slightly movable articulations of the arm, forearm, and hand united by dense fibrous connective tissue, permitting minimal to no motion
concepts.taPreferredLatin: Juncturae fibrosae partis liberae membri superioris
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Fibrous joints of free upper limb

concepts.description: Syndesmotic articulations connecting laryngeal cartilages via dense fibrous connective tissue, permitting minimal movement while maintaining structural rigidity of the laryngeal framework
Articulations where two or more bones meet, classified by structure and mobility, enabling skeletal movement, stability, and force transmission throughout the body

Synovial and fibrous articulations of the foot, encompassing the intertarsal, tarsometatarsal, intermetatarsal, metatarsophalangeal, and interphalangeal joints, collectively enabling locomotion and weight-bearing

concepts.canonicalName: Joints of free part of lower limb
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Joints of free part of lower limb
sourceObjectNames: Joints of free part of lower limb.g
concepts.description: Articulations of the lower extremity distal to the hip, encompassing the knee, ankle, and intrinsic foot joints, enabling weight-bearing, locomotion, and postural stability
Synovial and cartilaginous articulations connecting the humerus, radius, ulna, and hand bones, enabling the multiplanar mobility of the arm, forearm, and hand
Synovial articulations of the wrist and digits, encompassing the radiocarpal, intercarpal, carpometacarpal, metacarpophalangeal, and interphalangeal joints, enabling complex hand movements
Articulations connecting the bones of the pelvis, thigh, leg, and foot, enabling weight-bearing, locomotion, and stability through diverse synovial, cartilaginous, and fibrous joint types
Articulations connecting the clavicle and scapula to the thorax, comprising the sternoclavicular and acromioclavicular joints, enabling upper limb mobility and load transmission
Fibrous articulations of the cranial and facial bones, primarily sutures, that unite adjacent skull bones with minimal to no movement, providing structural rigidity and protection to the brain
Articulations connecting adjacent vertebrae via intervertebral discs and synovial facet joints, enabling spinal flexion, extension, rotation, and lateral bending while providing structural stability
Synovial articulations of the larynx connecting the thyroid, cricoid, and arytenoid cartilages, enabling vocal fold tension and abduction or adduction during phonation and respiration
Fibrous joints found exclusively in the skull where ossified edges are united by a thin layer of connective tissue, allowing minimal to no movement
A type of fibrous joint in which two bones are united by interosseous ligaments or membranes, permitting limited movement while maintaining structural stability
A freely movable joint characterized by a fluid-filled synovial cavity between articulating bones, enclosed by a fibrous capsule lined with synovial membrane

concepts.canonicalName: Synovial joints of free part of lower limb
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Synovial joints of free part of lower limb
sourceObjectNames: Synovial joints of free part of lower limb.g
concepts.description: Freely movable articulations of the lower extremity distal to the hip, including knee, ankle, and foot joints, characterized by synovial cavities enabling multiplanar locomotion and weight-bearing

concepts.canonicalName: Synovial joints of free upper limb
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Synovial joints of free upper limb
sourceObjectNames: Synovial joints of free upper limb.g
concepts.description: Freely movable articulations of the upper extremity distal to the shoulder girdle, including elbow, wrist, and hand joints, lined by synovial membrane and enclosed within fibrous capsules
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Synovial joints of free upper limb

concepts.description: Synovial articulations of the laryngeal skeleton, specifically the cricothyroid and cricoarytenoid joints, enabling phonation and airway regulation through controlled cartilaginous movement
Freely movable articulations of the pelvic ring, including the hip joints and pubic symphysis, enabling load transfer between the axial skeleton and lower limbs
Freely movable articulations of the thoracic cage, including costovertebral and sternoclavicular joints, lined with synovial membrane permitting gliding and rotational movements during respiration
Synovial and cartilaginous articulations of the thoracic cage connecting ribs to vertebrae and sternum, enabling respiratory movement and providing structural stability to the chest wall
Subdivision of the muscular system comprising muscles that enclose, support, and move the abdominopelvic region, including anterior, lateral, and posterior abdominal wall musculature

Fascial compartment of the arm containing the biceps brachii, brachialis, and coracobrachialis muscles, innervated by the musculocutaneous nerve, primarily responsible for elbow flexion and forearm supination

Fascial compartment of the forearm located on the flexor surface, housing muscles primarily responsible for wrist and finger flexion and forearm pronation, supplied mainly by the median and ulnar nerves
Subdivision of the muscular system comprising muscles located in the neck region, governing head movement, deglutition, phonation, and stabilization of cervical vertebrae
Subdivision of the muscular system comprising skeletal, smooth, and cardiac muscles located within the cranium, governing facial expression, mastication, ocular movement, and pharyngeal function

Subdivision of the muscular system comprising muscles located on the posterior aspect of the body, including paraspinal, gluteal, and posterior limb musculature, responsible for extension, rotation, and postural support
Skeletal muscles of the back located dorsal to the vertebral transverse processes, innervated by dorsal rami of spinal nerves, primarily responsible for spinal extension and postural support
A group of paired paraspinal muscles spanning the vertebral column that collectively extend, laterally flex, and stabilize the spine, comprising the iliocostalis, longissimus, and spinalis columns
concepts.shape: A broad, thick bilateral column of paravertebral muscle mass spanning the full length of the posterior trunk, tapering inferiorly toward the sacrum and iliac crest
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue, tendinous aponeurosis

arteries.text: Dorsal branches of posterior intercostal arteries
Dorsal branches of lateral sacral arteries

veins.text: Dorsal branches of posterior intercostal veins

nerves.text: Dorsal rami of cervical spinal nerves
Dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Dorsal rami of lumbar spinal nerves
Dorsal rami of sacral spinal nerves
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of cervical spinal nerves
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of lumbar spinal nerves

landmarks.text: Paravertebral muscular ridge palpable alongside spinous processes
Sacral origin palpable at posterior sacral surface and posterior superior iliac spine
Lateral border forming the medial boundary of the lumbar triangle of Petit
Tendinous intersections producing segmental bulges palpable along the thoracic spine

concepts.description: Layered connective tissue sheets of the abdominal wall that envelop muscles, compartmentalize structures, and provide pathways for nerves and vessels while supporting visceral organs
Ventral ramus-innervated muscles located inferior to the transverse processes of the vertebral column, primarily involved in trunk flexion and lateral bending
Intrinsic and extrinsic skeletal muscles acting on the foot that control toe movement, maintain plantar arches, and provide dynamic stability during stance and gait
Skeletal muscles of the cranial region grouped into facial expression, mastication, ocular, auricular, and scalp subsets, governing mimicry, jaw movement, and sensory organ function
Skeletal muscles of the lower extremity that enable locomotion, weight-bearing, and postural stability, organized into gluteal, thigh, leg, and foot compartments
Skeletal muscles of the cervical region that collectively support head movement, stabilize the spine, facilitate swallowing, and regulate laryngeal, hyoid, and mandibular positioning
Skeletal muscles lining the pelvic walls and floor that support pelvic viscera, facilitate continence, and enable hip and trunk movement
Skeletal muscles of the thoracic wall that govern respiration, stabilize the rib cage, and assist in trunk movement, including intercostals, diaphragm, and accessory respiratory muscles
Skeletal muscles spanning the shoulder girdle to the hand that enable mobility, stability, and fine motor control of the upper extremity
Organ system composed of skeletal, cardiac, and smooth muscle tissues that generates force and movement, maintains posture, and regulates internal organ function throughout the body
Collection of skeletal muscles spanning the hip, thigh, leg, and foot that enable locomotion, postural support, and joint stabilization of the lower extremity
Collection of skeletal muscles spanning the shoulder girdle, arm, forearm, and hand that execute movement, stabilize joints, and enable fine motor control of the upper extremity
Muscular floor of the pelvis formed primarily by the levator ani and coccygeus muscles, supporting pelvic viscera and maintaining continence
concepts.shape: Broad, funnel-shaped muscular sheet spanning the pelvic outlet, with a superior pelvic surface and inferior perineal surface, forming a concave hammock-like floor
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, endopelvic fascia, perineal fascia

nerves.text: Perineal branch of fourth sacral nerve

concepts.description: Muscular subdivision comprising skeletal muscles of the pelvic region, including pelvic floor, wall, and girdle muscles, which support pelvic viscera and facilitate locomotion and continence
Fascial compartment of the arm located on its dorsal aspect, containing the triceps brachii muscle and innervated by the radial nerve, primarily responsible for elbow extension
Fascial compartment on the dorsal aspect of the forearm housing extensor and supinator muscles, innervated primarily by the radial nerve, responsible for wrist and digit extension
Fascial compartment of the leg situated posterior to the interosseous membrane, housing superficial and deep muscle groups that plantarflex the foot and flex the toes, supplied by the tibial nerve
Group of intrinsic shoulder muscles originating on the scapula and inserting on the humerus, responsible for stabilizing the glenohumeral joint and producing arm movements
Posterior neck muscles connecting spinous processes to transverse processes, acting to extend and rotate the head and cervical vertebral column
Skeletal muscles situated in the outermost layer of the head, including facial expression and scalp muscles, responsible for mimicry, mastication, and cranial movement
Group of four paired muscles located superior to the hyoid bone that elevate the hyoid and floor of mouth during swallowing and depress the mandible
Subdivision of the muscular system comprising skeletal muscles of the thoracic wall, intercostal spaces, and diaphragm that facilitate respiration, posture, and trunk stability
Deep back muscles spanning from transverse to spinous processes of vertebrae, forming several layers that produce spinal rotation, extension, and stabilization
Protective and supportive structures of the eye, including the eyelids, conjunctiva, lacrimal apparatus, and extrinsic muscles, that facilitate ocular movement, lubrication, and protection

concepts.canonicalName: Autonomic division of peripheral nervous system
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Autonomic division of peripheral nervous system
sourceObjectNames: Autonomic division of peripheral nervous system.g
concepts.description: Division of the peripheral nervous system that regulates involuntary visceral functions—including cardiac muscle, smooth muscle, and glands—via sympathetic and parasympathetic subdivisions
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Autonomic division of peripheral nervous system

concepts.description: A nerve network formed by the ventral rami of C5–T1 spinal roots that innervates the upper limb, shoulder, and portions of the chest and back

Central organ of the nervous system housed within the cranial vault, comprising the cerebrum, cerebellum, and brainstem, responsible for processing sensory input, coordinating motor output, and regulating cognitive and autonomic functions

concepts.composition: gray matter, white matter, ependyma, choroid plexus, neuroglia, blood vessels, meninges

Inferior terminal branch of middle cerebral artery
Posterior temporal branches of posterior cerebral artery

nerves.text: Sympathetic plexus on internal carotid artery

concepts.description: Inferior region of the brain connecting the cerebrum to the spinal cord, comprising the midbrain, pons, and medulla oblongata, regulating vital autonomic functions

Inferior and superior colliculi of the tectum (corpora quadrigemina)

concepts.description: Division of the nervous system comprising the brain and spinal cord, enclosed within the skull and vertebral canal, responsible for integrating sensory input and coordinating motor output
Hindbrain structure located in the posterior cranial fossa that coordinates voluntary movement, maintains balance, and regulates muscle tone via cerebellar-cortical feedback loops

structure.text: Deep cerebellar nuclei (dentate, emboliform, globose, fastigial)

nerves.text: Dopaminergic fibers from ventral tegmental area

concepts.description: Paired brainstem structures forming the ventral midbrain, each consisting of the crus cerebri, substantia nigra, and tegmentum, transmitting major motor and sensory tracts
The largest part of the brain, comprising two hemispheres with a cortex responsible for higher cognitive functions, sensory processing, and voluntary motor control
concepts.shape: Large, ovoid bilateral mass with smooth outer convexity, deep medial fissure, inferior concave base, and extensively convoluted superolateral, medial, and inferior surfaces
concepts.composition: grey matter, white matter, ependyma, neuroglia, vascular connective tissue

nerves.text: Trigeminal sensory fibers to cerebral vessels

Cochlear ramus of the vestibulocochlear artery
Radiating arterioles of the basilar membrane

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular fibers from the internal carotid plexus

Caudal subdivision of the prosencephalon enclosing the thalamus, hypothalamus, epithalamus, and subthalamus, serving as a relay and regulatory center for sensory, autonomic, and endocrine functions

concepts.composition: gray matter, white matter, ependymal epithelium, neuroglia

concepts.shape: Thick, dense fibrous membranous sac enclosing the brain and spinal cord, with smooth inner and rough outer surfaces forming a tough elongated cylindrical tube
concepts.composition: dense irregular connective tissue, collagen fibers, fibroblasts, periosteum

Meningeal branch of internal carotid artery
Spinal meningeal branches of segmental arteries

Minor arterial circle of the iris

Cortical branches of posterior cerebral artery

Nerve network formed by the anterior rami of L1–S3 spinal nerves, innervating the lower limb, pelvis, and perineum
Caudal brainstem segment connecting the pons superiorly to the spinal cord, housing vital autonomic centers controlling respiration, heart rate, and blood pressure

concepts.description: Bundles of axons enclosed in connective tissue sheaths that transmit electrical impulses between the central nervous system and peripheral tissues, enabling sensory, motor, and autonomic functions
Organ system comprising the brain, spinal cord, peripheral nerves, and specialized sensory receptors that integrates stimuli, coordinates bodily functions, and enables perception
Division of the nervous system comprising all neural tissue outside the brain and spinal cord, including cranial nerves, spinal nerves, and autonomic ganglia
Innermost of the three meningeal layers, a delicate vascular membrane that directly adheres to the surface of the brain and spinal cord, following all gyri and sulci

Linea splendens along the anterior surface of the spinal cord

concepts.composition: gray matter, white matter, myelinated nerve fibers, unmyelinated nerve fibers, neuroglia, blood vessels

Collaterals from cranial nerve VIII (vestibulocochlear nerve)
Collaterals from cranial nerve IX (glossopharyngeal nerve)

Specialized organs of the nervous system that detect specific environmental stimuli—including light, sound, chemicals, and mechanical forces—and transduce them into neural signals
Cylindrical nervous tissue structure extending from the brainstem through the vertebral canal, transmitting sensory and motor signals between the brain and peripheral nervous system

Dorsal midbrain region containing the red nucleus, periaqueductal gray, cranial nerve nuclei (III, IV), and ascending/descending tracts, integral to motor coordination and pain modulation

Long circumferential branches of basilar artery

The most rostral division of the prosencephalon, comprising the cerebral hemispheres, basal ganglia, and limbic structures, responsible for higher cognitive, sensory, and motor functions

A central chamber of the inner ear that connects the cochlea anteriorly and the semicircular canals posteriorly, housing the utricle and saccule for equilibrium

landmarks.text: Opening of the aqueduct of the vestibule

Distinct structural and functional divisions of the human organism, classified regionally or systemically, enabling precise anatomical localization and clinical reference across disciplines

Topographic subdivisions of the cranium and face, delineating distinct surface areas—including frontal, parietal, occipital, temporal, and facial zones—used to localize anatomy and clinical pathology
Systematic topographic divisions of the human body surface and underlying structures used to localize anatomical landmarks, symptoms, and clinical findings for diagnostic and therapeutic purposes
Topographic subdivisions of the lower extremity that delineate surface areas including the gluteal, thigh, knee, leg, ankle, and foot regions for clinical and anatomical reference
Topographic subdivisions of the neck demarcating anterior, posterior, and lateral cervical triangles, used clinically to localize structures, lymph nodes, and pathology
Topographic subdivisions of the chest wall used to systematically localize surface anatomy, lesions, and clinical findings relative to underlying thoracic structures

Topographic subdivisions of the upper extremity that systematically divide it into named areas—shoulder, arm, elbow, forearm, wrist, and hand—facilitating precise anatomical description and clinical localization
Region of the lower limb encompassing the ankle joint, where the distal tibia and fibula articulate with the talus, bounded superiorly by the leg and inferiorly by the foot

concepts.description: Large paired pelvic bone formed by fusion of the ilium, ischium, and pubis, articulating with the sacrum posteriorly and the femur laterally

nerves.text: Periosteal and articular innervation is closely related to regional nerves, especially the axillary nerve near the surgical neck, the radial nerve along the posterior shaft, and the ulnar nerve near the medial epicondyle

concepts.description: A groove on the sternocostal surface of the heart marking the interventricular septum, housing the left anterior descending artery and great cardiac vein
Cardiac surface formed predominantly by the right ventricle, facing the sternum and costal cartilages, most susceptible to precordial trauma and pericardiocentesis access
The point at which the abdominal aorta divides into the left and right common iliac arteries, typically occurring at the level of the L4 vertebra
Semilunar valve located at the junction of the left ventricle and ascending aorta, comprising three cusps that prevent retrograde blood flow during diastole
Blunt, inferolateral tip of the heart formed primarily by the left ventricle, located in the fifth intercostal space at the midclavicular line, where the apical impulse is auscultated
Coronary vessels arising from the aortic sinuses that supply oxygenated blood to the myocardium, comprising the right and left coronary arteries with their respective branches

Venous vessels of the heart that drain deoxygenated blood from the myocardium, primarily emptying into the coronary sinus, which then returns blood to the right atrium
An anastomotic ring of arteries at the base of the brain, formed by the internal carotid, anterior and posterior cerebral, and communicating arteries, ensuring collateral cerebral perfusion

A circumferential groove on the external heart surface separating the atria from the ventricles, housing the coronary arteries, cardiac veins, and coronary sinus
Venous channels formed between the periosteal and meningeal layers of the dura mater that drain cerebral, cerebellar, and diploic veins into the internal jugular veins
Cardiac border extending nearly horizontally from the apex to the right, formed primarily by the right ventricle with a small contribution from the left ventricle
A groove on the diaphragmatic surface of the heart marking the boundary between the right and left ventricles, housing the posterior interventricular artery and cardiac vein

Vessels that carry oxygenated blood from the lungs to the left atrium, uniquely transporting arterial blood despite being classified as veins
Tricuspid cardiac valve located between the right atrium and right ventricle that prevents backflow of blood during ventricular systole

concepts.description: One of four cardiac chambers that receives deoxygenated systemic venous return via the superior and inferior venae cavae and coronary sinus, then directs blood through the tricuspid valve into the right ventricle

Posterior atrial branch of right coronary artery

concepts.description: Cardiac border formed primarily by the right atrium, extending from the superior to inferior vena cava, visible on chest X-ray and defining the right cardiac silhouette
Cardiac surface formed predominantly by the right atrium, facing the right lung and pericardium, visible in anterior and posterior mediastinal views
Muscular cardiac chamber that receives deoxygenated blood from the right atrium and pumps it into the pulmonary circulation via the pulmonary trunk

nerves.text: Thoracic cardiac branches of sympathetic trunk

concepts.description: Proximal segment of the aorta arising from the left ventricle, encompassing the aortic valve, sinuses of Valsalva, and sinotubular junction

concepts.canonicalName: Superior lobar artery of left lung

concepts.description: Branch of the left pulmonary artery supplying oxygenated blood to the superior lobe of the left lung, arising before or at the level of the upper lobe bronchus

Venous confluence where the left and right brachiocephalic veins merge posterior to the right first costal cartilage, forming the superior vena cava
Plane synovial joint between the acromion of the scapula and the lateral end of the clavicle, stabilized by acromioclavicular and coracoclavicular ligaments
Synovial joints formed between the distal row of carpal bones and the bases of the five metacarpals, enabling limited gliding motion critical to hand stability and grip function

Synchondroses and symphyses uniting the sternum with adjacent costal cartilages and sternal segments, providing structural rigidity while permitting limited respiratory movement
Cartilaginous joints uniting the anterior end of each rib to its corresponding costal cartilage, providing slight flexibility during respiration
Synovial plane joint formed between the articular tubercle of a rib and the transverse process of its corresponding thoracic vertebra, enabling gliding movements during respiration
Fibrous joints of the skull in which adjacent bones are united by dense connective tissue or ligaments, permitting minimal movement while maintaining rigid cranial integrity

Synovial ball-and-socket joint formed between the capitulum of the humerus and the head of the radius, permitting flexion, extension, and rotational forearm movements

Synovial plane joint where the head of a rib articulates with the costal facets of adjacent thoracic vertebral bodies and intervertebral disc

Synovial and cartilaginous articulations connecting the bones of the shoulder girdle, arm, forearm, and hand, enabling a wide range of upper extremity motion
Largest synovial joint in the body, formed by the femur, tibia, and patella, enabling flexion, extension, and slight rotation of the leg

Ligamentous complex on the outer ankle comprising the anterior talofibular, calcaneofibular, and posterior talofibular ligaments, stabilizing the joint against inversion stress
Fibrous connective tissue bands anchoring the epiglottis to adjacent laryngeal and hyoid structures, stabilizing its position during swallowing and phonation
Cartilaginous joint uniting the fifth lumbar vertebra to the sacrum via an intervertebral disc, providing limited mobility while transmitting axial loads between the spine and pelvis
Broad ligamentous complex on the medial ankle joining the medial malleolus to the talus, calcaneus, and navicular, resisting eversion and valgus stress
Pivot synovial joint between the dens of the axis and the anterior arch of the atlas, allowing rotational movement of the head

Condyloid synovial joints between the metatarsal heads and proximal phalanges of the toes, permitting flexion, extension, abduction, and adduction
A pivot synovial joint formed between the radial head and the radial notch of the ulna, enabling forearm pronation and supination
A fibrous joint uniting the radius and ulna along their interosseous borders via the interosseous membrane, permitting forearm pronation and supination
Synovial ellipsoid joint formed between the distal radius and proximal carpal row, permitting flexion, extension, abduction, and adduction of the wrist
Synovial joint between the auricular surfaces of the sacrum and ilium, transmitting axial load from the spine to the lower limbs with minimal mobility
Cartilaginous joint uniting the apex of the sacrum with the base of the coccyx, reinforced by anterior, posterior, and lateral sacrococcygeal ligaments, allowing limited flexion and extension
Synovial or fibrocartilaginous articulations where the costal cartilages of ribs 1–7 unite with the lateral facets of the sternum, permitting limited gliding movement during respiration

A cartilaginous joint in which two bones are united by fibrocartilage, permitting limited movement, as seen at the pubic symphysis and intervertebral discs
Freely movable articulations connecting the clavicle to the sternum and scapula, enabling upper limb mobility through the sternoclavicular and acromioclavicular joints
Synovial articulations between vertebral facets permitting gliding movements, stabilizing the spine while allowing flexion, extension, and rotation within segmental ranges
Synovial plane joints connecting the distal row of tarsal bones to the bases of the five metatarsals, forming the Lisfranc joint complex
Synovial condylar joint between the mandibular condyle and temporal bone's articular fossa, enabling jaw movements including depression, elevation, protrusion, and lateral excursion

concepts.shape: Elongated tubular synovial sleeves enclosing tendons, with smooth cylindrical inner and outer layers forming a closed double-walled sheath of variable length
concepts.composition: synovial membrane, dense irregular connective tissue, synovial fluid

landmarks.text: Proximal opening of the fibrous sheath at the metacarpophalangeal or metatarsophalangeal level
Annular pulleys overlying the sheath along the digits
Mesotenon attachment ridge on the dorsal aspect of the tendon

concepts.description: Synovial-lined fibrous tunnels encasing tendons of the leg and foot that reduce friction, maintain tendon alignment, and facilitate smooth gliding during locomotion
Synovial sleeves encasing tendons of the arm, forearm, and hand, reducing friction and enabling smooth gliding during movement
Dense connective tissue sheet spanning the thoracic and lumbar regions that envelops the deep back muscles and serves as a key attachment site for abdominal and gluteal musculature
concepts.shape: Broad, diamond-shaped fibrous sheet spanning the posterior thorax and lumbar region, with smooth anterior and posterior surfaces and tapered superior and inferior margins
concepts.composition: dense irregular connective tissue, collagen fibers, elastic fibers

arteries.text: Dorsal branches of posterior intercostal arteries
Medial branches of dorsal rami of lumbar arteries
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of lumbar arteries

nerves.text: Dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Dorsal rami of lumbar spinal nerves
Medial branches of dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Medial branches of dorsal rami of lumbar spinal nerves
Lateral branches of dorsal rami of lumbar spinal nerves

landmarks.text: Spinous process attachments of lumbar vertebrae
Twelfth rib attachment at superior border

concepts.description: Large, flat, superficial back muscle originating from the occiput and vertebral spinous processes (C7–T12), inserting on the scapular spine and clavicle, enabling scapular elevation, retraction, and rotation
concepts.shape: Large, flat, rhomboid-shaped muscle with a broad triangular profile, presenting superficial and deep surfaces spanning the posterior cervical and thoracic regions bilaterally
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense irregular connective tissue, tendinous aponeurosis

structure.text: Triangular aponeurosis at lower cervical and upper thoracic origin
Lateral tendon inserting onto the spine of the scapula, acromion, and clavicle

arteries.text: Superficial branch of transverse cervical artery

landmarks.text: Spine of the scapula (insertion ridge, palpable along posterior scapula)
Acromion process (lateral insertion point, most lateral bony prominence of shoulder)
External occipital protuberance (superior origin, palpable at occiput)
Spinous processes of C7–T12 (medial origin, palpable along posterior midline)
Posterior border of lateral third of clavicle (superior fiber insertion, palpable anterosuperiorly)

concepts.description: Three-headed muscle of the posterior arm compartment originating from the infraglenoid tubercle and posterior humerus, inserting onto the olecranon to extend the forearm at the elbow
concepts.shape: A large, fusiform muscle occupying the entire posterior compartment of the arm, with a broad, flat proximal region that tapers into a thick, common distal tendon, presenting posterior, anterior, medial, and lateral surfaces along its length.
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue, perimysium, epimysium

nerves.text: Nerve to long head of triceps brachii
Nerve to medial head of triceps brachii
Nerve to lateral head of triceps brachii

landmarks.text: Infraglenoid tubercle origin of long head
Spiral groove of humerus (radial nerve relationship)
Posterior shaft of humerus (medial head origin ridge)

concepts.description: Bony dilation at the anterior end of the anterior semicircular canal of the inner ear's osseous labyrinth, housing the membranous ampulla containing sensory hair cells for rotational equilibrium
Ventral gray matter region of spinal cord containing alpha and gamma motor neurons whose axons form ventral roots that innervate skeletal muscle

concepts.canonicalName: Anterior median fissure of medulla oblongata
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Anterior median fissure of medulla oblongata
concepts.description: A longitudinal midline groove on the ventral surface of the medulla oblongata, continuous with the anterior median fissure of the spinal cord, partially interrupted by decussating corticospinal fibers
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Anterior median fissure of medulla oblongata

concepts.description: The most anterior point of the external surface of the eyeball, located at the center of the cornea and representing the apex of its anterior curvature
Ventral divisions of lumbar spinal nerves L1–L5 that form the lumbar plexus, supplying motor and sensory innervation to the anterior thigh, groin, and medial leg
Bony labyrinth canal of the inner ear oriented in the sagittal plane, detecting angular head acceleration in the pitch (nodding) direction and contributing to vestibular equilibrium
concepts.shape: Narrow, arc-shaped bony tube oriented in a vertical plane, forming roughly two-thirds of a circle with a uniform circular cross-section and smooth outer and inner surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, periosteum, endosteum, hyaline cartilage remnants, perilymph-filled space, membranous labyrinth epithelium, endolymph

landmarks.text: Arcuate eminence of the petrous bone, marking the superior surface projection of the canal
Anterior ampullary opening into the vestibule
Common crus junction with the posterior semicircular canal
Petrous ridge orientation, used as a reference plane for canal positioning

concepts.description: Convex anterior surface of the cornea that interfaces with the tear film and external environment, serving as the primary refractive surface of the eye
Anterior surface of the iris facing the aqueous humor of the anterior chamber, characterized by crypts, ridges, and pigmentation patterns visible during slit-lamp examination
A longitudinal groove on the anterolateral surface of the medulla oblongata from which the hypoglossal nerve (CN XII) rootlets emerge
Middle layer of the three meningeal membranes surrounding the brain and spinal cord, characterized by its avascular, web-like structure overlying the subarachnoid space
concepts.shape: Thin, delicate, avascular, web-like membranous sheet forming a smooth, loosely fitting sac that conforms to the outer contours of the brain and spinal cord
concepts.composition: avascular fibrous connective tissue, collagen fibers, fibroblasts, mesothelial cells

landmarks.text: Arachnoid granulations along the superior sagittal sinus
Lumbar cistern at the L2–S2 vertebral level
Cisterna magna (cerebellomedullary cistern) at the posterior cranial fossa
Pontine cistern at the anterior surface of the pons

concepts.description: A telencephalic region located at the base of the forebrain containing cholinergic nuclei, including the nucleus basalis of Meynert, critical for memory, attention, and arousal
Basal region of the cochlea where the first turn begins, oriented toward the internal acoustic meatus and containing the high-frequency sound-processing region of the organ of Corti
Anterior region of the pons containing descending corticospinal and corticopontine fibers, pontine nuclei, and transverse pontocerebellar fibers connecting cerebral cortex to cerebellum

concepts.canonicalName: Branches of anterior part of lumbar plexus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Branches of anterior part of lumbar plexus
concepts.description: Neural branches arising from the anterior division of the lumbar plexus, primarily including the iliohypogastric, ilioinguinal, and genitofemoral nerves, supplying the lower abdomen and inguinal region
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Branches of anterior part of lumbar plexus

concepts.canonicalName: Branches of lateral cord of brachial plexus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Branches of lateral cord of brachial plexus
concepts.description: Neural branches arising from the lateral cord of the brachial plexus, comprising the musculocutaneous nerve, lateral root of median nerve, and lateral pectoral nerve
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Branches of lateral cord of brachial plexus

concepts.canonicalName: Branches of medial cord of brachial plexus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Branches of medial cord of brachial plexus
concepts.description: Nerve branches arising from the medial cord of the brachial plexus (C8–T1), including the medial pectoral, medial brachial cutaneous, medial antebrachial cutaneous, ulnar, and medial root of median nerves
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Branches of medial cord of brachial plexus

concepts.canonicalName: Branches of posterior cord of brachial plexus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Branches of posterior cord of brachial plexus
concepts.description: Neural branches arising from the posterior cord that supply motor and sensory innervation to the posterior compartments of the upper limb, including the axillary and radial nerves
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Branches of posterior cord of brachial plexus

concepts.canonicalName: Branches of posterior part of lumbar plexus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Branches of posterior part of lumbar plexus
concepts.description: Nerve branches arising from the dorsal divisions of lumbar plexus roots, supplying motor and sensory innervation to the anterior thigh and medial leg
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Branches of posterior part of lumbar plexus

concepts.description: Midbrain structures occupying the tegmentum and periaqueductal gray, coordinating motor control, sensory relay, and autonomic functions via cranial nerve nuclei and ascending-descending tracts
Core spinal cord region comprising the central canal, gray commissures, and surrounding gray matter that mediates sensory integration, motor coordination, and autonomic relay functions
Paired white matter tracts connecting the cerebellum to the brainstem, classified as inferior, middle, and superior, each conveying distinct afferent and efferent signals

veins.text: Vein of the lateral recess of the fourth ventricle

concepts.description: One of two symmetrical halves of the cerebrum, separated by the longitudinal fissure and comprising the cortex, white matter, and basal ganglia, responsible for higher neural functions
concepts.shape: Large ovoid mass with convex superolateral, flat medial, and irregular inferior surfaces, widest posteriorly, tapering anteriorly, with a smooth outer contour interrupted by sulci
concepts.composition: gray matter, white matter, ependyma, neuroglia, blood vessels

arteries.text: Cortical branches of anterior cerebral artery
Cortical branches of middle cerebral artery
Cortical branches of posterior cerebral artery

nerves.text: Dopaminergic fibers from ventral tegmental area
Cholinergic fibers from nucleus basalis of Meynert
Cholinergic fibers from medial septal nucleus

concepts.description: Spinal nerves C1–C8 emerging from the cervical spinal cord that innervate the neck, diaphragm, and upper extremities via the cervical and brachial plexuses
Nerve network formed by the anterior rami of C1–C4 spinal nerves, located deep to the sternocleidomastoid, supplying sensation to the neck and motor innervation to the diaphragm via the phrenic nerve
Apical region of the cochlea where the osseous spiral lamina and cochlear duct terminate, forming the blind end of the spiraling bony canal
White matter tracts connecting homologous cortical regions of the two cerebral hemispheres, enabling interhemispheric communication and coordination
Bony channel in the osseous labyrinth where the non-ampullated ends of the superior and posterior semicircular canals unite before opening into the vestibule
The most anterior point of the cornea, representing the geometric apex of its convex surface, used as a reference landmark in ophthalmic measurements and contact lens fitting
Transitional zone of the eye where the transparent cornea meets the opaque sclera, serving as a landmark for surgical incisions and the site of aqueous humor drainage structures
Outermost meningeal layer enclosing the brain, composed of dense fibrous connective tissue forming two fused periosteal and meningeal layers that separate to create venous dural sinuses
concepts.shape: Thick, dense fibrous membrane forming a smooth-surfaced, bilayered cranial vault lining with an outer periosteal and inner meningeal layer conforming to the interior cranial cavity contours
concepts.composition: dense irregular connective tissue, collagen fibers, elastic fibers, fibroblasts, endothelium

arteries.text: Meningeal branch of ascending pharyngeal artery
Meningeal branch of internal carotid artery
Meningeal branch of posterior inferior cerebellar artery

nerves.text: Meningeal branch of cervical spinal nerves
Sympathetic fibers from internal carotid plexus
Sympathetic fibers from middle meningeal plexus

landmarks.text: Crista galli attachment of falx cerebri
Internal occipital protuberance confluence of sinuses

concepts.description: Twelve paired peripheral nerves originating directly from the brain and brainstem that transmit sensory, motor, and autonomic signals to and from the head, neck, and viscera
Innermost layer of cranial meninges, a delicate vascular membrane that closely invests the brain surface and dips into sulci and fissures
concepts.shape: Delicate, translucent membranous sheet intimately conforming to the cerebral surface contours, forming a thin continuous investing layer over all gyri and sulci
concepts.composition: loose connective tissue, collagen fibers, elastic fibers, fibroblasts, pial cells, blood vessels

concepts.description: Parasympathetic division of the autonomic nervous system comprising cranial nerve outflow (CN III, VII, IX, X) and sacral spinal segments S2–S4, promoting rest-and-digest responses
A dorsal medullary elevation overlying the nucleus cuneatus, formed by ascending cuneate fasciculus fibers carrying fine touch and proprioception from the upper limb
Radially arranged smooth muscle of the iris, innervated by sympathetic fibers, that contracts to widen the pupil in response to low light or adrenergic stimulation
concepts.shape: Thin, flat annular sheet of radially arranged smooth muscle fibers forming a disc-like layer within the iris stroma, widest at the periphery and tapering centrally
concepts.composition: smooth muscle, myoepithelial cells, connective tissue

structure.text: Sympathetic nerve plexus within muscle sheet

arteries.text: Major arterial circle of the iris
Minor arterial circle of the iris

nerves.text: Sympathetic postganglionic fibers from the superior cervical ganglion

concepts.description: Basal ganglia subdivision comprising the caudate nucleus and putamen, involved in motor control, habit formation, and reward-based learning
Ciliated epithelial lining cells that form the inner surface of the brain's ventricular system and central canal of the spinal cord, regulating cerebrospinal fluid production and flow
Outermost division of the ear comprising the auricle and external acoustic meatus, functioning to collect and direct sound waves toward the tympanic membrane
concepts.shape: Funnel-shaped fibrocartilaginous structure with a curved, irregular rim and a concave medial surface that tapers into a narrow tubular canal directed anteromedially
concepts.composition: elastic cartilage, dense irregular connective tissue, skin, adipose tissue, skeletal muscle, fibrous tissue

structure.text: Cartilaginous portion of external acoustic meatus
Osseous portion of external acoustic meatus

arteries.text: Anterior auricular branches of superficial temporal artery

concepts.description: Spherical sensory organ of the visual system housed within the orbit, containing refractive media and a light-sensitive retina that transduces visual stimuli into neural signals
concepts.shape: Roughly spherical organ approximately 24 mm in diameter, with a slightly more curved anterior pole where the transparent cornea protrudes, and a flattened posterior pole where the optic nerve exits, giving the globe a mildly prolate form
concepts.composition: dense fibrous connective tissue, transparent avascular connective tissue, smooth muscle, neural tissue, vascular tissue, pigmented epithelium, stratified squamous epithelium, aqueous humor, vitreous humor, crystalline lens

structure.text: Vascular tunic (uvea), comprising the choroid, ciliary body, and iris

arteries.text: Major arterial circle of the iris
Minor arterial circle of the iris

nerves.text: Sympathetic fibers from internal carotid plexus

landmarks.text: Optic disc (posterior exit point of the optic nerve)
Ora serrata (anterior boundary of the retina)

concepts.description: Circumferential ridges and furrows on the anterior iris surface that accommodate changes in pupillary diameter during constriction and dilation
Largest cerebral lobe, located anterior to the central sulcus, governing voluntary motor control, executive function, speech production, and personality
A dorsal medullary elevation formed by the nucleus gracilis, transmitting fine touch, vibration, and proprioception from the lower limb via the gracile fasciculus
Neural tissue rich in neuronal cell bodies, dendrites, and unmyelinated fibers, forming cortical layers and deep nuclei throughout the brain and spinal cord

arteries.text: Cortical branches of middle cerebral artery
Cortical branches of anterior cerebral artery
Cortical branches of posterior cerebral artery

concepts.description: Neuronal cell body-rich tissue within the medulla oblongata containing cranial nerve nuclei and autonomic centers that regulate vital functions including respiration, cardiac rate, and reflex activity

arteries.text: Perforating branches of anterior spinal artery

concepts.description: Neuronal cell body-rich region within the dorsal pontine tegmentum containing cranial nerve nuclei, reticular formation, and ascending/descending tract relay centers

veins.text: Vein of the lateral recess of the fourth ventricle

concepts.description: Caudal region of the brainstem's medulla oblongata containing the pyramidal decussation and housing vital autonomic centers controlling respiration and cardiovascular function

arteries.text: Lateral medullary branches of posterior inferior cerebellar artery
Medial medullary branches of anterior spinal artery
Posterior medullary branches of posterior spinal artery

concepts.description: Peripheral nerve network located below the clavicle, comprising the cords and terminal branches of the brachial plexus that innervate the upper limb
Cerebral cortical region buried within the lateral sulcus, forming the floor of the Sylvian fissure and involved in interoception, autonomic regulation, and visceral sensation
Grooves on the cerebral surface that separate adjacent lobes of the brain, serving as boundaries used in neurological localization and clinical brain mapping
Gray matter region between the dorsal and ventral horns, containing interneurons and the intermediolateral cell column that mediates autonomic and reflex integration

veins.text: Coronal venous plexus of spinal cord

concepts.composition: compact bone, periosteum, hyaline cartilage, connective tissue, epithelium, perilymph, endolymph, sensory neuroepithelium

landmarks.text: Crista ampullaris of each semicircular duct

concepts.description: System of ocular structures comprising the lacrimal gland, puncta, canaliculi, lacrimal sac, and nasolacrimal duct responsible for tear production and drainage
concepts.shape: A compact, curved drainage system forming a crescent-like arc along the superomedial and medial orbital rim, with a small ovoid glandular mass and a narrow tubular passage
concepts.composition: serous glandular epithelium, stratified columnar epithelium, stratified squamous epithelium, connective tissue, smooth muscle, fibrous tissue

nerves.text: Sympathetic fibers from superior cervical ganglion

landmarks.text: Lacrimal punctum (superior and inferior), visible at medial end of each eyelid margin
Lacrimal fossa, bony depression in superolateral orbit housing the lacrimal gland
Lacrimal groove, vertical sulcus on the medial orbital wall formed by the lacrimal bone and maxilla
Anterior lacrimal crest, ridge on frontal process of maxilla marking the anterior boundary of the nasolacrimal fossa
Posterior lacrimal crest, ridge on lacrimal bone marking the posterior boundary of the nasolacrimal fossa

A white matter column of the medulla oblongata situated between the anterolateral and posterolateral sulci, containing ascending and descending tracts including the spinothalamic and rubrospinal pathways
Bony labyrinth canal of the inner ear oriented in the horizontal plane, detecting angular acceleration during head rotation about a vertical axis
concepts.shape: A horizontally oriented, toroidal arc of bony canal forming roughly 270° of a circle, with a smooth curved tubular lumen and flattened superior and inferior surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, periosteum, endosteum, perilymph, endolymph, hyaline cartilage matrix remnants, simple squamous epithelium, connective tissue

structure.text: Common crus (shared non-ampullated limb opening into utricle)

landmarks.text: Arcuate eminence of the lateral canal (subtle bony prominence on petrous bone)
Utricular opening of the non-ampullated limb

concepts.description: A cortical region forming the medial border of each cerebral hemisphere, comprising the cingulate, parahippocampal, and subcallosal gyri, integral to emotion, memory, and autonomic regulation

nerves.text: Cholinergic fibers from nucleus basalis of Meynert
Cholinergic fibers from medial septal nucleus
Dopaminergic fibers from ventral tegmental area
Thalamocortical fibers from anterior thalamic nuclei
Thalamocortical fibers from mediodorsal thalamic nucleus

concepts.description: Third and largest branch of the trigeminal nerve carrying both sensory and motor fibers, innervating the lower jaw, teeth, tongue, and muscles of mastication
Air-filled cavity within the temporal bone containing the ossicles that transmit and amplify sound vibrations from the tympanic membrane to the oval window of the inner ear
A peripheral nerve containing both afferent sensory and efferent motor fibers, enabling simultaneous transmission of sensory input and motor commands between the CNS and target tissues
Posterior cerebral lobe situated at the occipital bone that serves as the primary visual cortex, processing and integrating visual information received from the retinas
A midbrain nuclear complex in the periaqueductal gray at the level of the superior colliculus that innervates most extraocular muscles and the sphincter pupillae via cranial nerve III
Oval prominence on the anterolateral medulla oblongata, overlying the inferior olivary nucleus, involved in motor coordination and cerebellar modulation
Imaginary line passing through the center of curvature of the cornea and lens to the fovea centralis, representing the primary geometric axis of the eye
Cerebral lobe situated between the frontal and occipital lobes, responsible for processing somatosensory information, spatial awareness, and sensory integration
Bony dilation at the posterior end of the posterior semicircular canal of the osseous labyrinth, housing the membranous ampulla and its sensory crista ampullaris
Dorsal gray matter projection of the spinal cord that receives and processes incoming sensory (afferent) signals, including pain, temperature, and touch

concepts.canonicalName: Posterior median sulcus of medulla oblongata
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Posterior median sulcus of medulla oblongata
concepts.description: A shallow midline groove on the dorsal surface of the medulla oblongata that marks the boundary between the paired posterior funiculi and overlies the posterior median septum
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Posterior median sulcus of medulla oblongata

concepts.description: The point on the posterior surface of the eyeball representing its most posterior aspect, located slightly lateral to the optic disc and corresponding to the fovea centralis

Posterior divisions of the five lumbar spinal nerves that supply the muscles, joints, and skin of the lower back, with medial branches innervating deep paraspinal muscles and lateral branches providing cutaneous sensation
A bony labyrinth canal oriented in a vertical plane perpendicular to the petrous ridge, detecting angular head acceleration in the sagittal-oblique plane
concepts.shape: Vertically oriented, arc-shaped bony canal forming roughly two-thirds of a circle, with a narrow tubular lumen, positioned obliquely in the petrous temporal bone
concepts.composition: compact bone, periosteum, endosteum, perilymph, endolymph, membranous labyrinth epithelium, connective tissue

structure.text: Common crus (shared with anterior semicircular canal)

landmarks.text: Inferior ampullary opening into the vestibule
Common crus opening on the posterior wall of the vestibule
Posterior wall of the petrous part of the temporal bone adjacent to the canal

concepts.description: Inner concave surface of the cornea forming the anterior boundary of the anterior chamber, lined by corneal endothelium and interfacing with aqueous humor
Posterior-facing surface of the iris directed toward the lens and posterior chamber, lined by a double layer of pigmented epithelium that blocks stray light transmission
A longitudinal groove on the posterolateral surface of the medulla oblongata from which the glossopharyngeal, vagus, and accessory nerve rootlets emerge
White matter tracts connecting the cerebral cortex to subcortical structures, including the thalamus, brainstem, and spinal cord, transmitting afferent and efferent signals
Circular aperture in the center of the iris that regulates light entry into the eye, varying in diameter via iris musculature in response to light intensity and autonomic innervation
Inner border of the iris that encircles and defines the pupillary aperture, forming the free edge through which light enters the eye
Paired ventral ridges of the medulla oblongata formed by corticospinal tract fibers descending from the motor cortex, where most fibers decussate to control contralateral voluntary movement

arteries.text: Paramedian branches of the basilar artery
Deep perforating branches of the anterior spinal artery

concepts.description: A longitudinal sulcus on the lateral medulla oblongata, posterior to the olive, through which the glossopharyngeal, vagus, and accessory nerve rootlets emerge
Nerve plexus formed by the anterior rami of L4–S3, located on the posterior pelvic wall, supplying motor and sensory innervation to the pelvis, perineum, and lower limb
Afferent nerve that transmits sensory impulses from receptors in peripheral tissues to the central nervous system, conveying modalities such as pain, temperature, touch, and proprioception
A bony semicircular canal segment lacking a fusion with another canal, opening independently into the vestibule via a single unrejoined osseous aperture
Circular smooth muscle of the iris, innervated by parasympathetic fibers via CN III, that constricts the pupil in response to light and accommodation
concepts.shape: A thin, flat, ring-shaped band of smooth muscle approximately 0.75–1 mm wide encircling the pupillary margin of the iris, with anterior and posterior surfaces oriented parallel to the plane of the iris and an inner free edge bordering the pupil
concepts.composition: smooth muscle

structure.text: Muscle bundles arranged in concentric circular fascicles
Connective tissue stroma investing the muscle ring
Sphincter–dilator interface zone at the peripheral border

arteries.text: Major arterial circle of the iris
Minor arterial circle of the iris

landmarks.text: Collarette of the iris (inner boundary of the ciliary zone)
Peripheral border abutting the dilator pupillae

concepts.description: Mixed peripheral nerve formed by the union of dorsal (sensory) and ventral (motor) roots exiting the vertebral column through intervertebral foramina
Bony helical channel within the cochlea of the inner ear that winds approximately 2.75 turns around the modiolus, housing the membranous cochlear duct and auditory sensory structures
A shallow circumferential groove at the corneoscleral junction marking the boundary between the transparent cornea and the opaque sclera
Rostral segment of the brainstem's most caudal division, continuous with the pons superiorly, housing cranial nerve nuclei and vital autonomic centers controlling cardiorespiratory function

arteries.text: Lateral medullary branches of vertebral artery
Medial medullary branches of vertebral artery
Lateral medullary branches of posterior inferior cerebellar artery

concepts.description: Nerve branches arising proximal to the clavicle from the roots and trunks of the brachial plexus, supplying muscles of the shoulder girdle and back
Proximal division of the brachial plexus comprising roots and trunks located above the clavicle, giving rise to branches innervating shoulder girdle muscles
Dorsal midbrain region forming the roof of the cerebral aqueduct, comprising the superior and inferior colliculi, which coordinate visual and auditory reflexes respectively

arteries.text: Collicular branches of posterior cerebral artery

concepts.description: Cerebral lobe located inferior to the lateral sulcus, involved in auditory processing, language comprehension, and memory via the hippocampus and Wernicke's area
Spinal nerves arising from thoracic vertebral levels T1–T12 that innervate intercostal muscles, thoracic and abdominal wall structures, and convey sensory information from overlying dermatomes
Sympathetic division of the autonomic nervous system originating from preganglionic neurons in spinal cord segments T1–L2, mediating fight-or-flight responses

A dorsolateral medullary elevation overlying the spinal trigeminal nucleus and tract, visible on the posterior surface of the brainstem between the cuneate tubercle and the inferior cerebellar peduncle
Autonomic subdivision that exclusively regulates involuntary functions of internal organs, glands, and smooth muscle through sympathetic and parasympathetic pathways
Boundaries of the C-shaped CSF-filled cavity formed by the corpus callosum superiorly, caudate nucleus and thalamus medially, and corona radiata laterally
Regions of the CNS composed primarily of myelinated axons and glial cells that transmit signals between gray matter areas, appearing white due to lipid-rich myelin sheaths

arteries.text: Corpus callosum branches of anterior cerebral artery
Internal capsule branches of anterior choroidal artery

concepts.description: Myelinated nerve fibers within the diencephalon that form tracts connecting thalamic nuclei to cortical and subcortical regions, facilitating relay of sensory, motor, and limbic signals
Peripheral region of myelinated axons surrounding the gray matter that transmits ascending sensory and descending motor signals between the brain and body
Body region between the thorax and pelvis, bounded superiorly by the diaphragm and inferiorly by the pelvic inlet, housing digestive, urinary, and reproductive organs
Lateral cartilaginous flare of the external nose that forms the rounded inferolateral wall of each nostril, contributing to nasal airway patency and facial aesthetics
Region of the upper limb extending from the elbow to the wrist, bounded proximally by the cubital fossa and distally by the radiocarpal joint, divided into anterior and posterior compartments
Cutaneous region of the anterior thigh extending from the inguinal fold superiorly to the knee, overlying the quadriceps musculature and femoral neurovascular structures
Superficial region of the anterior thoracic wall bounded superiorly by the clavicles, inferiorly by the costal margins, and laterally by the anterior axillary lines, overlying the thoracic viscera
The most distal, rounded tip of the external nose, forming its most anteriorly projecting point and overlying the lower lateral cartilages
Cutaneous region of the lateral head centered on the auricle, bounded superiorly by the temporal region and inferiorly by the parotid region
Region of the upper limb extending from the shoulder to the elbow, encompassing the anterior and posterior compartments of the arm between the axilla and cubital fossa
Superficial anatomical region of the wrist corresponding to the eight carpal bones, bounded proximally by the forearm and distally by the metacarpus
Fleshy facial wall forming the lateral boundary of the oral cavity, composed of skin, buccinator muscle, and mucosa, contributing to mastication and facial expression
Anterior prominence of the lower face formed by the mental protuberance of the mandible, defining the inferior contour of the face below the lower lip
Anterior region of the elbow joint where the brachial artery bifurcates, bounded by the epicondyles proximally, containing the cubital fossa and key neurovascular structures
The superior ridge of the external nose extending from the root to the apex, forming the visible midline profile between the nasal bones and upper lateral cartilages
Thin fold of stratum corneum epidermis that extends from the proximal nail fold onto the dorsal nail plate surface, sealing the space to prevent infection
concepts.shape: A thin, crescent-shaped fold of keratinized epidermis that extends from the proximal nail fold onto the dorsal surface of the nail plate, forming a smooth, translucent rim at the proximal margin of the nail
concepts.composition: keratinized stratified squamous epithelium, stratum corneum

lymphatics.text: Superficial lymphatic vessels of the hand

concepts.description: Visible facial prominence composed of bone, cartilage, and soft tissue forming the external framework of the nasal airway and housing the nares
concepts.shape: Pyramidal projection of variable size with a superior root, inferior apex, two lateral surfaces, and a flattened base, forming an irregular triangular prism on the midface
concepts.composition: hyaline cartilage, elastic cartilage, compact bone, fibrofatty tissue, skeletal muscle, skin, mucous membrane, sebaceous glands

arteries.text: Alar branches of lateral nasal artery

concepts.description: Paired movable folds of skin and connective tissue bordering the anterior orbit that protect the ocular surface and distribute the tear film
concepts.shape: Paired thin, horizontally oriented tegumental folds of ovoid contour, presenting anterior cutaneous and posterior conjunctival surfaces, forming an elliptical aperture when open
concepts.composition: skin, subcutaneous connective tissue, skeletal muscle, smooth muscle, dense fibrous tissue (tarsal plate), elastic cartilage, mucous membrane (conjunctiva), sebaceous glands, meibomian glands, eccrine sweat glands

concepts.description: Distal edge of the nail plate that extends beyond the hyponychium, representing the unattached margin where the nail separates from the underlying fingertip soft tissue
Posterior region of the hip overlying the gluteal muscles and ilium, bounded by the iliac crest superiorly and the gluteal fold inferiorly, important for intramuscular injections
Keratinized filamentous appendages of the skin arising from follicles in the dermis, providing sensory function, thermoregulation, and protection across body surfaces

concepts.composition: keratinized epithelium, keratin protein, melanocytes, connective tissue

landmarks.text: Hair follicle opening (pore) at the epidermal surface

concepts.description: Eight interlocking flat and irregular bones forming the neurocranium that encloses and protects the brain, comprising the frontal, parietal, temporal, occipital, sphenoid, and ethmoid bones
Paired bony structures comprising the clavicle and scapula that connect the upper limb to the axial skeleton and provide muscular attachment points
Paired bony structures comprising the ilium, ischium, and pubis that unite anteriorly at the pubic symphysis and posteriorly with the sacrum to form the pelvic ring
Axial skeletal structures comprising 33 vertebrae arranged in cervical, thoracic, lumbar, sacral, and coccygeal regions, forming the structural backbone that protects the spinal cord
Groove on the superior surface of the calcaneus that, with the talus, forms the sinus tarsi, housing the interosseous talocalcaneal ligament
