Medial ridge of the iliac crest's superior border that serves as the attachment site for the transversus abdominis muscle and the thoracolumbar fascia
Rough, non-articular region on the proximal tibial plateau situated between the medial and lateral condyles, serving as the attachment site for cruciate ligaments and menisci
Bony projection on the proximal tibial plateau, situated between the medial and lateral condyles, serving as an attachment site for cruciate ligaments and menisci
A deep posterior groove on the distal femur situated between the medial and lateral condyles, housing the cruciate ligaments within the knee joint
A transverse bony ridge on the posterior distal femur that separates the intercondylar fossa from the popliteal surface, serving as an attachment site for the posterior joint capsule
Gap between two adjacent ribs containing intercostal muscles, nerves, and vessels, bounded superiorly and inferiorly by the costal grooves of neighboring ribs
Paired bony ridges on the posterior sacral surface formed by fused articular processes of sacral vertebrae, located between the median and lateral sacral crests
Region of the iliac crest located between the inner and outer lips, serving as an attachment site for the transversus abdominis muscle
Bony prominence on the inner surface of the occipital bone marking the convergence of the cruciform eminence and attachment site of the falx cerebri
Concave inner cranial surface bearing the sagittal sulcus superiorly, meningeal grooves, and granular foveolae, forming part of the calvaria's inner table

concepts.canonicalName: Internal surface of squamous part of frontal bone
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Internal surface of squamous part of frontal bone
concepts.description: Concave inner bony surface forming the anterior cranial fossa floor's anterosuperior wall, bearing impressions for cerebral gyri, meningeal grooves, and the sagittal sulcus superiorly
Sharp medial ridge of the radius extending from the radial tuberosity to the ulnar notch, serving as the attachment site for the interosseous membrane of the forearm
Sharp medial ridge of the tibial shaft that runs between the anterior and posterior surfaces, serving as the attachment site for the interosseous membrane connecting the tibia and fibula
Sharp medial ridge of the ulnar shaft that provides attachment for the interosseous membrane connecting the ulna to the radius
Sharp medial ridge of the fibula that provides attachment for the interosseous membrane connecting the fibula to the tibia
A bony ridge on the posterior femur connecting the greater and lesser trochanters, serving as an attachment site for the quadratus femoris muscle

A bony ridge on the anterior femur extending obliquely between the greater and lesser trochanters, serving as the attachment site for the iliofemoral ligament and marking the junction between the femoral neck and shaft
A groove on the anterior humerus between the greater and lesser tubercles that houses the tendon of the long head of the biceps brachii

Bony aperture formed between adjacent vertebral pedicles that transmits spinal nerve roots, dorsal root ganglia, and radicular blood vessels, and is a common site of neural compression in degenerative disease
Paired bony openings formed between adjacent vertebral pedicles that transmit spinal nerve roots and accompanying vessels, serving as clinically significant sites of nerve compression
Flat superior or inferior bony surface of a vertebral body that articulates with an intervertebral disc, facilitating load transmission and spinal flexibility
A small bony projection of the occipital bone that subdivides the jugular foramen into medial and lateral compartments, separating the sigmoid sinus from cranial nerves IX, X, and XI
A sharp bony projection of the ischium that separates the greater and lesser sciatic notches, serving as a landmark for pudendal nerve block and pelvic floor assessment
Bony prominence of the inferior ischium that bears body weight during sitting and serves as the attachment site for hamstring muscles and sacrotuberous ligament
Bony bar of the pelvic girdle formed by the conjoined inferior pubic ramus and ramus of the ischium, bordering the obturator foramen inferiorly
Inferior and posterior bone of the three pelvic components, forming the lower acetabulum and bearing the ischial tuberosity, which supports body weight during sitting
concepts.shape: Irregular, L-shaped bone forming the posteroinferior portion of the pelvis, with flat medial and lateral surfaces, a rough posterior border, and a thickened inferior margin
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage

arteries.text: Periosteal branches of the obturator artery
Periosteal branches of the inferior gluteal artery
Acetabular branch of the obturator artery

nerves.text: Sympathetic periosteal nerve fibers from the inferior hypogastric plexus

concepts.description: A notch on the lateral occipital bone that, paired with the jugular notch of the temporal bone, forms the jugular foramen transmitting the IJV, CN IX, X, and XI
Bony projection of the occipital bone extending laterally from the posterior condylar region, forming the posterior boundary of the jugular foramen
Broad posterior quadrilateral plate of the cricoid cartilage, taller than its arch, forming the dorsal foundation of the laryngeal skeleton
Paired quadrilateral cartilaginous plate forming the lateral wall of the thyroid cartilage, with left and right laminae fusing anteriorly to create the laryngeal prominence
Paired flat bony plate forming the posterior vertebral arch, extending from each pedicle to the midline spinous process, enclosing the vertebral foramen posteriorly
Hyaline and elastic cartilaginous structures forming the rigid framework of the larynx, protecting the airway and enabling phonation through vocal fold movement
Anterior midline projection of the thyroid cartilage formed by the fusion of its two laminae, more pronounced in males due to androgenic influence on laryngeal growth
The thickest and most laterally positioned angle of the scapula, bearing the glenoid cavity that articulates with the humeral head to form the glenohumeral joint
Bony ridge of the humerus extending from the greater tubercle to the lateral epicondyle, separating the anterior and posterior surfaces and serving as attachment for the lateral intermuscular septum
Thickest border of the scapula extending from the glenoid cavity to the inferior angle, serving as attachment for teres major, teres minor, and the long head of triceps brachii
Distal lateral projection of the femur that articulates with the lateral tibial plateau and lateral patellofacial surface, forming the lateral compartment of the knee joint
Proximal tibial articular eminence on the lateral side that bears the lateral meniscus and articulates with the lateral femoral condyle to form the lateral tibiofemoral joint
Bony projection on the lateral aspect of the distal femur, proximal to the lateral condyle, serving as an attachment site for the fibular collateral ligament and lateral head of the gastrocnemius
Bony projection on the lateral distal humerus serving as the origin for forearm extensor muscles and the site of pathology in lateral epicondylitis
Bony projection on the lateral aspect of the intercondylar eminence of the tibia, serving as an attachment point for the lateral meniscus and posterior cruciate ligament
Bony ridge on the posterolateral femoral shaft forming the outer border of the linea aspera, serving as attachment for the vastus lateralis and short head of biceps femoris
Articular surface on the lateral aspect of the talus that articulates with the medial surface of the lateral malleolus of the fibula, forming part of the ankle mortise
Bony projection forming the distal lateral end of the fibula that articulates with the talus, serving as the lateral stabilizing landmark of the ankle mortise joint

Paired bony column of the atlas vertebra (C1) situated between the superior and inferior articular facets, transmitting skull-to-spine compressive loads and anchoring the atlanto-occipital and atlantoaxial joints
Paired lateral bony component of the occipital bone flanking the foramen magnum, bearing the occipital condyles that articulate with the atlas and the hypoglossal canal
Paired lateral bony mass of the sacrum flanking the sacral foramina, formed by fused costal and transverse elements, bearing the auricular surface for sacroiliac articulation

nerves.text: Lateral branches of sacral dorsal rami
Medial branches of sacral dorsal rami
Sympathetic fibers from sacral sympathetic trunk

concepts.description: Bony projection forming the outer segment of a vertebral transverse process, serving as a muscle and ligament attachment site and derived from the costal element

arteries.text: Dorsal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Lateral branch of dorsal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Lateral branch of dorsal branch of lumbar artery

veins.text: Dorsal branch of posterior intercostal vein

concepts.description: Bony projection of the calcaneal tuberosity extending laterally, serving as an attachment site for the abductor digiti minimi and the plantar fascia
A bony projection of the malleus that extends laterally to attach to the tympanic membrane, creating the visible malleolar prominence seen through the eardrum
Bony projection on the lateral aspect of the talus that articulates with the fibula and calcaneus, commonly fractured in inversion ankle injuries
Bony projection of the sphenoid bone forming the posterior boundary of the pterygomaxillary fissure, serving as the origin for the medial and lateral pterygoid muscles

Paired bony ridges on the posterolateral sacrum representing fused transverse process tubercles, lateral to the intermediate crests, serving as attachment sites for the sacrotuberous and sacrospinous ligaments
A bony ridge on the lateral aspect of the distal femoral shaft, extending proximally from the lateral epicondyle, serving as an attachment site for the lateral intermuscular septum

Bony ridge on the lateral aspect of the distal humerus, extending proximally from the lateral epicondyle and serving as an attachment site for the brachioradialis and extensor carpi radialis longus muscles
Bony surface of the fibular shaft facing laterally, bordered by the anterior and posterior borders, serving as the origin for the peroneus longus and brevis muscles
Convex bony surface of the radial shaft facing laterally, providing attachment for the supinator muscle proximally and contributing to forearm contour
Flat bony surface of the tibial shaft facing laterally, bordered by the anterior and interosseous borders, providing attachment for the tibialis anterior muscle
Bony prominence on the posterior process of the talus, lateral to the flexor hallucis longus tendon groove, serving as an attachment point for the posterior talofibular ligament
Small medial projection of the incus that articulates with the head of the stapes, forming the incudostapedial joint within the middle ear ossicular chain
Small paired bony projection of the hyoid bone that articulates at the junction of the body and greater horn, serving as an attachment site for the stylohyoid ligament
Bony cavity inferior to the pelvic inlet, bounded by the sacrum, coccyx, and ischiopubic bones, containing pelvic viscera and forming the birth canal

Bony aperture inferior to the greater sciatic foramen, bounded by the sacrospinous and sacrotuberous ligaments, transmitting the obturator internus tendon, pudendal nerve, and internal pudendal vessels

A small concave notch on the posterior border of the ischium, inferior to the ischial spine, converted into a foramen by the sacrospinous and sacrotuberous ligaments to transmit the obturator internus tendon
A bony projection on the posteromedial femur at the junction of the neck and shaft, serving as the insertion point for the iliopsoas muscle
Bony projection on the anterior surface of the proximal humerus that serves as the insertion site for the subscapularis muscle of the rotator cuff
Paired bony projection of the sphenoid bone forming the posterior orbital roof and anterior cranial fossa floor, separated from the greater wing by the superior orbital fissure
Bony ridge on the superior surface of the sphenoid body that forms the anterior border of the chiasmatic sulcus, separating it from the jugum sphenoidale
A prominent longitudinal ridge on the posterior surface of the femoral shaft serving as a key attachment site for multiple thigh muscles and intermuscular septa
Bony ridge forming the boundary between the greater and lesser pelvis, composed of the pubic crest, pectineal line, arcuate line, and sacral promontory
Tooth surface facing the tongue, comprising the lingual aspect of both anterior and posterior teeth, relevant in occlusal assessment and restorative dentistry
Elongated inferior projection of the incus that descends parallel to the malleus handle and articulates with the stapes via the lenticular process
Five large vertebrae of the lower back, distinguished by their massive bodies, absence of costal facets, and transverse foramina, positioned between the thoracic vertebrae and sacrum
concepts.shape: Large, kidney-shaped vertebral bodies with broad flat superior and inferior surfaces, thick cylindrical form, wide transverse width, and a triangular vertebral foramen bounded by sturdy cortical walls
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, hyaline cartilage, fibrocartilage

nerves.text: Posterior primary rami of lumbar spinal nerves
Medial branches of posterior rami of lumbar spinal nerves
Gray rami communicantes of lumbar sympathetic trunk
Perivascular sympathetic plexus of lumbar arteries

concepts.description: Crescent-shaped articular cartilage lining the acetabulum's inner surface that contacts the femoral head, forming the weight-bearing region of the hip joint
A concave depression on the medial surface of the lateral malleolus of the fibula that articulates with the lateral facet of the talus during ankle joint formation
A shallow vertical groove on the posterior surface of the lateral malleolus that serves as a passageway for the tendons of the peroneus longus and brevis muscles

Lateral edge of the occipital bone that articulates with the mastoid part of the temporal bone, forming the occipitomastoid suture posterolateral to the foramen magnum
A groove on the medial surface of the mastoid process of the temporal bone that serves as the attachment site for the posterior belly of the digastric muscle
Bony projection of the temporal bone located posterior to the external auditory meatus, serving as an attachment site for sternocleidomastoid and other neck muscles
Curved bony ridge of the maxilla bearing the upper teeth, forming the superior component of the dental occlusion and defining the upper jaw's dental contour
Bony surface of the sphenoid's greater wing facing the pterygopalatine fossa, transmitting the foramen rotundum and articulating with the maxilla
Sharp ridge along the medial aspect of the fibular shaft that faces the tibia and provides attachment for the interosseous membrane separating the anterior and posterior compartments
Longitudinal ridge along the medial aspect of the humeral shaft extending from the lesser tubercle to the medial epicondyle, providing attachment for the medial intermuscular septum

Longest border of the scapula, extending vertically between the superior and inferior angles, oriented parallel to the vertebral column and serving as an attachment site for the serratus anterior muscle
Sharp subcutaneous ridge of the tibial shaft extending from the medial condyle to the posterior border of the medial malleolus, palpable throughout its length
Medial articular prominence at the distal femur that forms the medial tibiofemoral joint, larger and more inferiorly positioned than the lateral condyle
Proximal tibial bony prominence on the medial side that articulates with the medial femoral condyle and supports the medial meniscus
Bony projection on the medial aspect of the distal femur, superior to the medial condyle, serving as an attachment site for the medial collateral ligament and adductor magnus tendon
Bony prominence on the medial distal humerus serving as the attachment site for forearm flexor muscles and the ulnar collateral ligament
Bony projection on the medial aspect of the intercondylar eminence of the tibial plateau, serving as an attachment point for cruciate ligaments and menisci
A bony ridge on the posteromedial femoral shaft forming the inner border of the linea aspera, serving as an attachment site for the vastus medialis and adductor muscles
Articular surface on the medial aspect of the talus that articulates with the medial malleolus of the tibia, contributing to the stability of the ankle mortise joint
Bony prominence forming the medial aspect of the ankle mortise, comprising the distal medial projection of the tibia, serving as an attachment site for the deltoid ligament

Bony projection forming the larger, inferomedial prominence of the calcaneal tuberosity that serves as the primary weight-bearing surface and attachment site for the plantar fascia and intrinsic foot muscles
Bony projection of the sphenoid bone forming the medial wall of the pterygoid fossa, serving as the attachment site for the medial pterygoid muscle
A bony ridge on the posterior femur extending superiorly from the medial epicondyle, serving as an attachment site for the medial head of gastrocnemius and vastus medialis
Bony ridge on the distal humerus forming the medial border of the shaft proximal to the medial epicondyle, serving as an attachment site for the medial intermuscular septum
Smooth cartilaginous face of the arytenoid directed medially toward its contralateral counterpart, forming the posterior boundary of the intercartilaginous glottic space
Narrow bony surface of the fibular shaft facing the interosseous membrane and tibia, bounded by the anterior and interosseous borders
Subcutaneous flat bony surface along the anteromedial shaft of the tibia, bordered by the anterior and medial borders, palpable beneath the skin throughout its length
Smooth bony surface of the ulnar shaft facing the medial aspect of the forearm, subcutaneous throughout its length and palpable between the olecranon and styloid process
Bony projection on the posterior aspect of the talus, located medial to the flexor hallucis longus tendon groove, serving as an attachment point for the deltoid ligament
Unpaired bony ridge formed by fusion of sacral spinous processes, running longitudinally along the posterior midline of the sacrum
A paired bony aperture on the anterolateral surface of the mandible, typically below the second premolar, transmitting the mental nerve and vessels
Bony triangular elevation at the midline of the anterior mandible forming the characteristic prominence of the human chin
Paired bony prominence on the anteroinferior mandible forming the lateral ends of the mental protuberance, contributing to chin contour

Styloid process of the third metacarpal base

concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, articular cartilage, periosteum

Periosteal branches of plantar metatarsal arteries

Tuberosity of the first metatarsal (plantar aspect of base)
Intermetatarsal facets on the lateral and medial surfaces of the bases

Cartilaginous projection of the arytenoid cartilage extending anterolaterally, serving as the attachment site for the posterior and lateral cricoarytenoid muscles

Lateral crus of greater alar cartilage

veins.text: Intraosseous venous plexus of femoral neck

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular plexus of medial circumflex femoral artery
Sympathetic perivascular plexus of lateral circumflex femoral artery

nerves.text: Recurrent articular branch of common fibular nerve

Narrowed cylindrical segment of the radius situated between the radial head and the radial tuberosity, enclosed by the annular ligament to permit forearm rotation
Narrow constricted segment of a rib connecting the head to the tubercle, positioned anterior to the transverse process of the corresponding thoracic vertebra

Periosteal branch of posterior intercostal artery

veins.text: Dorsal branch of posterior intercostal vein

nerves.text: Dorsal ramus of thoracic spinal nerve

concepts.description: Narrow constriction of the scapula situated between the glenoid cavity laterally and the body of the scapula medially, serving as an attachment region for joint capsule structures
Constricted region of the talus connecting the head to the body, oriented slightly medially, vulnerable to fracture and avascular necrosis due to its tenuous blood supply
Constricted cervical region of a tooth where the crown meets the root, marked by the cementoenamel junction and serving as the attachment site for the gingival margin
Bony cranial compartment that encases and protects the brain, comprising the calvaria and cranial base, distinct from the viscerocranium forming the facial skeleton

Frontal branch of superficial temporal artery
Mastoid branch of posterior auricular artery
Petrosal branch of middle meningeal artery

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular plexus of middle meningeal artery

concepts.description: A bony ridge or linear elevation on a skeletal surface that runs at a diagonal angle, serving as an attachment site for muscles and defining structural boundaries

Elongated depression on the superior articular surface of the arytenoid cartilage that provides attachment for the vestibular ligament and thyroarytenoid muscle
A bony ridge on the superior pubic ramus extending from the pubic tubercle to the acetabulum, forming the anterior boundary of the obturator foramen
Large aperture in the anterior hip bone formed by the pubis and ischium, largely closed by the obturator membrane, through which the obturator vessels and nerve pass

Serrated border of the temporal bone that articulates with the occipital bone along the lambdoid suture, forming part of the posterolateral cranial vault

A posterior depression on the distal humerus that accommodates the olecranon process of the ulna during full elbow extension
Narrow orifice in the floor of the frontal sinus that communicates with the middle meatus of the nasal cavity via the frontonasal duct, enabling mucus drainage

Thin horizontal bony plate of the frontal bone forming the roof of the orbit and floor of the anterior cranial fossa, separating orbital and cranial cavities
Triangular bony surface of the frontal bone forming the roof of the orbit and floor of the anterior cranial fossa, separated by the ethmoidal notch medially
Smooth, quadrilateral bony surface of the sphenoid's greater wing forming the lateral wall of the orbit, bounded by the inferior and superior orbital fissures

Superior border of the greater wing of the sphenoid bone that articulates with the parietal bone, forming part of the pterion region of the lateral skull
Narrow isthmus of the vertebral arch connecting the superior and inferior articular processes, commonly the site of stress fractures causing spondylolysis

Sharp bony ridge on the superior ramus of the pubis forming the lateral boundary of the pelvic inlet and serving as an attachment site for the pectineal ligament
A bony ridge on the posterior femur extending from the lesser trochanter to the linea aspera, serving as the attachment site for the pectineus muscle
Bony ring forming the proximal attachment of the upper limb to the axial skeleton, comprising the clavicle and scapula bilaterally

Paired bony projections forming the lateral walls of the vertebral foramen, connecting the vertebral body to the laminae and serving as key landmarks in posterior spinal surgery and pedicle screw placement

arteries.text: Spinal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Spinal branch of lateral sacral artery
Spinal branch of ascending cervical artery
Spinal branch of deep cervical artery

concepts.description: Inferior subdivision of the abdominopelvic cavity, bounded by the pelvic bones, sacrum, and coccyx, enclosing the bladder, rectum, and internal reproductive organs
Bony ring formed by the two hip bones, sacrum, and coccyx that connects the vertebral column to the lower limbs and supports the pelvic viscera
Bony ring marking the superior opening of the true pelvis, bounded by the sacral promontory, arcuate lines, pubic rami, and symphysis pubis
The inferior opening of the pelvic cavity bounded by the coccyx, sacrotuberous ligaments, ischial tuberosities, pubic arch, and symphysis pubis through which pelvic structures exit
Concave anterior surface of the sacrum facing the pelvic cavity, marked by four transverse ridges and paired anterior sacral foramina transmitting sacral nerve ventral rami
Dense pyramidal bone region forming the skull base, housing the inner ear structures, carotid canal, and cranial nerve VII and VIII pathways

arteries.text: Petrosal branch of middle meningeal artery

nerves.text: Jacobson's nerve (tympanic branch of glossopharyngeal nerve)
Sympathetic plexus of internal carotid artery

concepts.description: Small tubular bones of the toes, comprising proximal, middle, and distal rows in digits two through five, and only proximal and distal in the hallux
Long bones of the digits forming the fingers, arranged in proximal, middle, and distal rows, with the thumb lacking a middle phalanx
Bony projection on the inferior surface of the basilar part of the occipital bone that serves as the attachment point for the pharyngeal raphe
Triangular posterior femoral area bounded by medial and lateral supracondylar lines, forming the floor of the popliteal fossa superior to the intercondylar line
Bony arc forming the dorsal portion of the first cervical vertebra, connecting the lateral masses and bearing a posterior tubercle in place of a spinous process

concepts.canonicalName: Posterior articular facet of dens axis
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Posterior articular facet of dens axis
concepts.description: Smooth articular surface on the posterior aspect of the dens of C2, forming the synovial joint with the transverse ligament of the atlas
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Posterior articular facet of dens axis

concepts.description: A longitudinal bony ridge on the fibula extending between the styloid process and posterior aspect of the lateral malleolus, separating the posterior and lateral compartment muscle attachments
A longitudinal bony ridge along the dorsal aspect of the radial shaft, extending from the radial tuberosity to the posterior aspect of the styloid process
A sharp subcutaneous ridge of the ulna extending from the olecranon to the styloid process, palpable throughout its length and serving as an attachment for the deep fascia of the forearm
Largest of three talar articular surfaces on the calcaneus, located on its superior aspect, forming the primary weight-bearing articulation of the subtalar joint
Bony projection of the dorsum sellae of the sphenoid bone that serves as an attachment point for the tentorium cerebelli and borders the sella turcica posteriorly
A bony ridge on the gluteal surface of the ilium extending from the iliac crest to the greater sciatic notch, serving as the posterior attachment boundary for the gluteus maximus muscle
Bony projection on the posterior iliac surface, located inferior to the posterior superior iliac spine, serving as an attachment point for the posterior sacroiliac ligament
Roughened region on the posterior tibial plateau, between the medial and lateral condyles, that provides attachment for the posterior cruciate ligament and posterior meniscal horns

Bony projection forming the posterior rim of the tibial plafond, stabilizing the ankle mortise and preventing posterior talar displacement
Bony projection on the posterior margin of the obturator groove of the pubis, serving as an attachment point for the posterior obturator membrane
Bony projection extending posteriorly from the talus, divided into medial and lateral tubercles by the groove for the flexor hallucis longus tendon
Four paired openings on the dorsal sacral surface through which the posterior rami of sacral spinal nerves S1–S4 exit

Bony projection on the posterior ilium marking the superiormost point of the posterior iliac crest, palpable beneath the skin dimple and serving as a key landmark for sacroiliac joint and spinal procedures
Concave triangular surface of the arytenoid cartilage facing posteriorly, providing attachment for the transverse and oblique arytenoid muscles involved in vocal fold adduction
Posterior bony surface of the fibula bounded by the interosseous and posterior borders, featuring the medial crest and housing the origin of the flexor hallucis longus and tibialis posterior muscles
Bony surface forming the dorsal aspect of the humeral shaft, featuring the radial groove transmitting the radial nerve and profunda brachii artery
Cranial fossa surface of the petrous temporal bone facing posteriorly into the posterior cranial fossa, bearing the internal acoustic meatus and subarcuate fossa
Bony surface of the radius facing posteriorly, marked by a ridge dividing extensor muscle attachments and palpable along the dorsal forearm
Dorsal aspect of the flat triangular bone of the pectoral girdle, divided by the spine of the scapula into the supraspinous and infraspinous fossae
Posterior bony surface of the tibia characterized by the soleal line, which serves as the origin for the soleus muscle and marks the path of neurovascular structures
Subcutaneous bony surface of the ulna facing dorsally, forming a palpable ridge from olecranon to styloid process and serving as attachment for the extensor carpi ulnaris
Teeth located distal to the canines, comprising premolars and molars, primarily functioning in grinding and crushing food during mastication
concepts.shape: Broad, multi-cusped crowns with occlusal, buccal, lingual, mesial, and distal surfaces; wider mesiodistally than anteriors, with flattened to ridged occlusal tables suited for grinding
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

structure.text: Occlusal surface with cusps and fossae

nerves.text: Dental plexus of superior alveolar nerves
Dental plexus of inferior alveolar nerve

concepts.description: Bony midline projection on the posterior arch of C1 vertebra, representing a rudimentary spinous process and serving as an attachment point for the ligamentum nuchae and rectus capitis posterior minor muscle
Bony projection on the posterior aspect of a cervical vertebra's transverse process, serving as an attachment site for posterior cervical muscles and distinguishing it from the anterior tubercle
A surface of the fibular shaft facing posteriorly and medially, bordered by the interosseous and medial crests, providing attachment for the flexor hallucis longus and tibialis posterior muscles
Bony projection forming the anterior upper margin of the sacrum where it meets L5, creating a landmark used in obstetrics to assess pelvic inlet dimensions
Bony prominence on the lateral mid-shaft of the radius serving as the insertion site for the pronator teres muscle, facilitating forearm pronation
Bony channel in the sphenoid bone connecting the foramen lacerum to the pterygopalatine fossa, transmitting the nerve and vessels of the pterygoid canal
A bony depression on the sphenoid bone located between the medial and lateral pterygoid plates, serving as the origin for the medial pterygoid muscle
A hook-shaped bony projection extending inferiorly from the medial pterygoid plate of the sphenoid bone, serving as a pulley for the tensor veli palatini tendon
A notch on the sphenoid bone where the inferior pterygoid process diverges, accommodating the pyramidal process of the palatine bone

Paired bony projection of the sphenoid bone extending inferiorly from the junction of its body and greater wings, consisting of medial and lateral plates that form key boundaries of the pterygopalatine and infratemporal fossae
A bony projection arising from the posterior edge of the lateral pterygoid plate of the sphenoid bone, serving as an attachment point for the pterygospinous ligament
Bony arch formed by the convergence of the inferior pubic rami at the subpubic angle, demarcating the inferior boundary of the pelvic outlet
Bony ridge on the superior surface of the pubic body, extending medially to the pubic tubercle, serving as an attachment site for the rectus abdominis and inguinal ligament
Bony prominence on the superior surface of the pubic bone, serving as the medial attachment of the inguinal ligament and a key landmark for hernia assessment
Anteromedial paired bone of the hip forming the pubic symphysis, comprising a body, superior ramus, and inferior ramus, contributing to the acetabulum and delimiting the obturator foramen

arteries.text: Pubic branch of inferior epigastric artery

veins.text: Pubic tributary of inferior epigastric vein

concepts.description: Small bony elevation on the intertrochanteric crest of the femur that serves as the insertion point for the quadratus femoris muscle
A shallow depression on the anterior surface of the lateral humeral epicondyle that accommodates the radial head during full elbow flexion
A spiral channel on the posterior humeral shaft that transmits the radial nerve and deep brachial artery, serving as a key surgical landmark where nerve injury causes wrist drop
A concave articular depression on the lateral aspect of the coronoid process of the ulna that articulates with the circumference of the radial head, forming the proximal radioulnar joint

Bony projection on the lateral distal radius that extends more distally than the ulnar styloid, serving as an attachment site for the brachioradialis muscle and lateral collateral ligament of the wrist
Bony prominence on the medial aspect of the proximal radius, just distal to the radial neck, serving as the insertion site for the biceps brachii tendon
Bony projection of the ischium that extends anteriorly and superiorly to join the inferior pubic ramus, forming the ischiopubic ramus and defining the lower boundary of the obturator foramen
Curved flat bone of the thoracic cage that articulates posteriorly with thoracic vertebrae and contributes to thoracic wall protection and respiratory mechanics
concepts.shape: Elongated, curved flat bone with a C-shaped arc, presenting smooth external convex and roughened internal concave surfaces, tapering from a broad posterior end to a narrow anterior cartilaginous tip
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, hyaline cartilage, articular cartilage, red bone marrow

concepts.description: The terminal tip of a tooth's root where the apical foramen is located, serving as the exit point for neurovascular structures entering the pulp canal
Portion of a tooth embedded within the alveolar bone, anchored by the periodontal ligament, extending from the cementoenamel junction to the apex

arteries.text: Dental branches of inferior alveolar artery
Dental branches of anterior superior alveolar artery
Dental branches of posterior superior alveolar artery
Periodontal branches of inferior alveolar artery

nerves.text: Dental branches of inferior alveolar nerve

concepts.description: Tubular osseous channel within the sacrum, formed by fusion of sacral vertebral foramina, that transmits the cauda equina, sacral nerve roots, and the filum terminale
Inferior opening in the posterior sacral canal formed by the non-fusion of the S4–S5 laminae, serving as the landmark for caudal epidural anesthesia
Paired bony projections flanking the sacral hiatus, representing rudimentary inferior articular processes of the fifth sacral vertebra, serving as palpable landmarks for caudal epidural anesthesia
Rough bony prominence on the posterior lateral surface of the sacrum serving as the attachment site for the posterior sacroiliac ligaments
Medial iliac surface comprising the iliac tuberosity, auricular surface for sacroiliac articulation, and pelvic surface facing the lesser pelvis
Medial edge of the parietal bone that articulates with its contralateral counterpart along the sagittal suture at the cranial vault midline
A concave hollow on the medial pterygoid plate of the sphenoid bone that serves as the origin for the tensor veli palatini muscle
A small notch on the superior border of the scapula, medial to the coracoid process, bridged by the superior transverse scapular ligament through which the suprascapular nerve passes
Bony saddle-shaped depression in the sphenoid bone's body that houses the pituitary gland, bounded anteriorly by the tuberculum sellae and posteriorly by the dorsum sellae
Bony partition within the frontal sinus that divides it into right and left halves, often asymmetrically positioned relative to the midline

nerves.text: Sympathetic periarterial plexus of anterior ethmoidal artery

concepts.description: A bony midline partition within the sphenoidal sinuses that divides the two cavities, often asymmetrically, and may deviate laterally, influencing surgical access during transsphenoidal procedures

arteries.text: Posterior septal branches of sphenopalatine artery

concepts.description: Bony framework of the lower extremity distal to the hip girdle, comprising the femur, patella, tibia, fibula, tarsal bones, metatarsals, and phalanges
Bony framework of the upper extremity excluding the shoulder girdle, comprising the humerus, radius, ulna, and bones of the wrist and hand
A diagonal bony ridge on the posterior tibial surface, extending from the fibular facet to the medial border, serving as the proximal attachment for the soleus muscle
Anteroinferior angle of the parietal bone that articulates with the greater wing of the sphenoid and frontal bone at the pterion, a clinically significant region prone to epidural hemorrhage
Narrow vertical bony ridge on the anterior surface of the sphenoid body that articulates with the perpendicular plate of the ethmoid, contributing to the nasal septum
A slender bony projection on the lateral margin of the carotid sulcus of the sphenoid bone, forming the medial border of the foramen lacerum
Serrated border of the frontal bone that articulates with the greater wing of the sphenoid, forming part of the anterolateral cranial floor boundary
Bony plate of the sphenoid bone connecting the two lesser wings anteriorly, forming the smooth, flat surface superior to the prechiasmatic sulcus
A prominent bony ridge projecting posteriorly from the dorsal surface of the scapula, dividing it into supraspinous and infraspinous fossae and extending laterally to form the acromion
A sharp bony projection of the greater wing of the sphenoid, located at its posteroinferior angle, serving as an attachment point for the sphenomandibular ligament
A shallow concavity on the posterior scapular neck between the base of the spine of the scapula and the glenoid rim, through which the suprascapular nerve and vessels pass
Posterior bony projection of a vertebra extending from the junction of the two laminae, serving as an attachment site for muscles and ligaments of the back
Beveled inferior margin of the parietal bone that articulates with the squamous part of the temporal bone, forming the squamosal suture along the lateral cranial wall
Serrated bony border of the sphenoid's greater wing that articulates with the squamous part of the temporal bone, forming part of the pterion region
Broad, vertically oriented plate of the frontal bone forming the forehead and contributing to the anterior cranial vault, articulating laterally with the parietal and sphenoid bones

arteries.text: Anterior branch of middle meningeal artery
Periosteal branches of superficial temporal artery

concepts.description: Flat, curved plate of the occipital bone located posterior and superior to the foramen magnum, forming the posteroinferior cranial vault and providing attachment for nuchal muscles and ligaments

nerves.text: Periosteal branches of greater occipital nerve

concepts.description: Thin, flat portion of the temporal bone forming the lateral cranial wall, articulating with the parietal bone superiorly and contributing to the temporal fossa
A bony landmark at the manubriosternal joint where the sternum angulates slightly anteriorly, marking the level of the second rib, aortic arch, and tracheal bifurcation
Smooth, oval articular facet on the medial end of the clavicle that articulates with the manubrium sterni and first costal cartilage, forming the sternoclavicular joint
Medial end of the clavicle that articulates with the manubrium sterni at the sternoclavicular joint, forming the only bony connection between the upper limb and axial skeleton
Flat axial bone forming the anterior midline of the thoracic cage, articulating with the clavicles and costal cartilages of ribs 1–7, comprising the manubrium, body, and xiphoid process
concepts.shape: Flat, elongated dagger-shaped bone with anterior and posterior surfaces, wider superiorly and tapering inferiorly, presenting slightly convex anterior and concave posterior surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, red bone marrow, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage

arteries.text: It is supplied mainly by perforating branches of the internal thoracic arteries with contribution from adjacent anterior intercostal branches

veins.text: Venous drainage follows the arterial pattern into the internal thoracic and anterior intercostal veins

nerves.text: Its periosteum and overlying region receive sensory supply from adjacent anterior cutaneous branches of the intercostal nerves

concepts.description: A slender bony projection of the temporal bone extending inferiorly from its inferior surface, serving as an attachment site for muscles and ligaments of the tongue, pharynx, and hyoid bone

concepts.canonicalName: Styloid process of third metacarpal bone
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Styloid process of third metacarpal bone
concepts.description: A bony projection on the radial aspect of the third metacarpal base that articulates with the capitate and serves as an attachment site for the extensor carpi radialis brevis tendon
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Styloid process of third metacarpal bone

concepts.description: Bony prominence on the medial epicondyle of the humerus serving as the attachment site for the anterior bundle of the ulnar collateral ligament
Shallow depression on the inner surface of the mandible, superior to the mylohyoid line, that accommodates the sublingual salivary gland
Bony angle formed at the pubic symphysis by the convergence of the inferior pubic rami, measuring ~70–75° in males and ~90–100° in females, reflecting pelvic sexual dimorphism
Concave depression on the anterior (costal) surface of the scapula that serves as the origin for the subscapularis muscle
A bony groove on the inferior surface of the temporal and sphenoid bones that lodges the cartilaginous portion of the auditory tube
Bony ridge on the frontal bone superior to each orbit, housing the frontal sinus and serving as an attachment site for facial musculature
Bony landmark of the scapula located at the junction of the medial and superior borders, positioned at approximately the level of the first or second thoracic vertebra
Smooth articular surface on the superior aspect of a vertebral arch's zygapophyseal process that articulates with the inferior facet of the vertebra above
Paired bony projections at the superior sacral end that articulate with the inferior articular processes of L5, forming the lumbosacral zygapophyseal joints
Bony projection extending superiorly from the vertebral arch, bearing a facet that articulates with the inferior articular process of the vertebra above
Paired concave articular facets on the superior aspect of the atlas that articulate with the occipital condyles, forming the atlanto-occipital joints permitting nodding movements

concepts.canonicalName: Superior articular surface of lateral condyle
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Superior articular surface of lateral condyle
concepts.description: Cartilage-covered proximal tibial facet on the lateral condyle that articulates with the lateral femoral condyle, forming the lateral compartment of the tibiofemoral joint
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Superior articular surface of lateral condyle

concepts.description: Paired concave articular facets on the proximal tibial plateau that articulate with the medial and lateral femoral condyles to form the tibiofemoral joint
Thin, sharp border of the scapula extending from the superior angle to the coracoid process, featuring the suprascapular notch through which the suprascapular nerve passes
Articular depression on the superior posterolateral vertebral body that forms the costovertebral joint with the head of the numerically corresponding rib
Smooth articular surface on the dorsal aspect of the talus that forms the talocrural joint by articulating with the distal tibia, transmitting axial load during weight-bearing
Paired cartilaginous projection extending superiorly from the thyroid cartilage lamina, connecting via the lateral thyrohyoid ligament to the greater horn of the hyoid bone
Small bony projection on the internal surface of the mandibular symphysis that serves as the superior attachment point for the genioglossus muscle
A bony ridge on the occipital bone extending laterally from the external occipital protuberance, serving as an attachment site for trapezius, sternocleidomastoid, and splenius capitis muscles
Bony projection of the pubic bone extending superolaterally from the pubic body to the iliopubic eminence, forming the anterior boundary of the obturator foramen

arteries.text: Pubic branch of inferior epigastric artery
Corona mortis (anastomotic branch between obturator and inferior epigastric arteries)

veins.text: Pubic branch of inferior epigastric vein

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular plexus of obturator artery

concepts.description: A curved bony ridge on the external surface of the parietal bone marking the upper limit of the temporalis muscle attachment and origin of the temporal fascia

Narrow oval opening at the top of the thoracic cage bounded by T1 vertebra posteriorly, first ribs laterally, and manubrium anteriorly, transmitting major neurovascular structures between neck and thorax
A V-shaped midline indentation on the superior border of the thyroid cartilage that separates the two superior thyroid laminae and serves as a palpable anterior neck landmark
A bony prominence on the superior margin of the thyroid cartilage's oblique line, marking the upper attachment point of the sternothyroid muscle
A shallow concavity on the superior aspect of each vertebral pedicle that, paired with the inferior notch of the vertebra above, forms the intervertebral foramen
A bony ridge on the posterior aspect of the ulna, extending from the radial notch, serving as the inferior attachment site for the supinator muscle
A shallow bony groove on the ilium located immediately superior to the acetabular rim, serving as an attachment site for the reflected head of the rectus femoris muscle
Bony projection on the superior margin of the glenoid cavity of the scapula that serves as the origin of the long head of the biceps brachii muscle
Shallow concave depression on the posterior scapula located superior to the spine of the scapula, housing the supraspinatus muscle
A bony ridge on the lateral surface of the radius just proximal to the styloid process, serving as an attachment site for the brachioradialis muscle
A bony ridge on the outer occipital surface, superior to the superior nuchal line, marking the highest attachment point for the nuchal ligament and epicranial aponeurosis

Constricted region of the proximal humerus just distal to the greater and lesser tubercles, commonly fractured and named for its surgical significance in axillary nerve and anterior circumflex humeral artery injury risk
A medial bony shelf of the calcaneus that supports the talus superiorly, provides attachment for the spring ligament, and forms part of the tarsal tunnel floor
Medial articular facet of the pubic body that articulates with its contralateral counterpart via fibrocartilage at the pubic symphysis, a secondary cartilaginous joint of the pelvis
A groove on the inferior talus between the anterior and posterior articular surfaces that, with the calcaneal sulcus, forms the sinus tarsi
Seven short bones forming the posterior foot, comprising the calcaneus, talus, navicular, cuboid, and three cuneiforms, transmitting body weight between leg and metatarsals
concepts.shape: Seven irregularly shaped, short bones of varying sizes forming a compact, interlocking mosaic with flat, convex, and concave articular surfaces on multiple faces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, articular cartilage, periosteum

Deltoid branch of posterior tibial artery

lymphatics.text: Deep lymphatic vessels of the foot

landmarks.text: Calcaneal tuberosity (posterior prominence of the calcaneus)
Sustentaculum tali (medial shelf of the calcaneus supporting the talus)
Groove for peroneus longus tendon on the cuboid bone

concepts.description: A cone-shaped osseoligamentous canal located between the talus and calcaneus, housing the interosseous talocalcaneal ligament and neurovascular structures
Thin bony plate of the petrous temporal bone forming the roof of the tympanic cavity, separating the middle ear from the middle cranial fossa
Bony ridge on the lateral skull marking the superior boundary of the temporal fossa and serving as the attachment site for the temporalis fascia and muscle
Concave bony surface on the lateral frontal bone that forms the anterior wall of the temporal fossa, providing attachment for the temporalis muscle
Concave lateral surface of the sphenoid's greater wing forming the medial wall of the temporal fossa, providing attachment for the temporalis muscle
One of 12 mid-spinal vertebrae distinguished by costal facets for rib articulation, a heart-shaped body, and long downward-sloping spinous processes

concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, fibrocartilage

Spinal branches of posterior intercostal arteries
Periosteal branches of posterior intercostal arteries

nerves.text: Recurrent meningeal branches of spinal nerves
Dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves
Medial branches of dorsal rami of thoracic spinal nerves

concepts.description: Twelve vertebrae of the mid-spine distinguished by costal facets for rib articulation, forming the posterior thoracic cage and providing limited rotational mobility
concepts.shape: Heart-shaped vertebral bodies with lateral costal facets, long posteriorly angled spinous processes, and a narrow cylindrical vertebral foramen forming a mid-sized column segment
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, fibrocartilage

arteries.text: Dorsal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Spinal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Periosteal branches of posterior intercostal arteries

nerves.text: Periosteal nerve fibers from intercostal nerves
Medial branch of thoracic dorsal ramus

Transverse costal facets on the transverse processes
Superior and inferior vertebral notches forming the intervertebral foramina

landmarks.text: Oblique line on the external surface of each lamina
Inferior horn articulating facet for cricoid cartilage

concepts.description: Proximal articular surface of the tibia forming two condylar facets that articulate with the femoral condyles, bearing compressive loads during weight-bearing
Bony prominence on the anterior proximal tibia serving as the insertion point for the patellar ligament, transmitting quadriceps muscle forces to extend the knee
Articular depression on the transverse process of thoracic vertebrae T1–T10 that articulates with the tubercle of the corresponding rib
Bony projection on the medial surface of the atlas's lateral mass that anchors the transverse ligament, securing the dens of the axis and stabilizing the atlantoaxial joint

Horizontal bony ridges on the anterior sacral surface marking fusion lines between the five sacral vertebral bodies
A rough ridge on the inferior surface of the clavicle's lateral third that serves as the attachment site for the trapezoid ligament of the coracoclavicular complex
Small triangular depression on the anterior surface of the arytenoid cartilage, located superior to the oblong fovea, serving as an attachment site for the vestibular ligament
A deep pit on the medial surface of the greater trochanter of the femur that serves as the insertion site for the obturator externus muscle
Pulley-shaped articular surface on the medial distal humerus that articulates with the trochlear notch of the ulna, forming the hinge component of the elbow joint
Pulley-shaped articular surface at the distal end of a proximal or middle foot phalanx that forms a hinge joint with the adjacent distal phalanx
Pulley-shaped articular surface at the distal end of a proximal or middle phalanx of the hand that forms a hinge joint with the adjacent phalanx distally
Pulley-shaped articular surface on the superior talus that articulates with the distal tibia and fibula, transmitting body weight and enabling dorsiflexion and plantarflexion
Small depression on the anteromedial surface of the orbital plate of the frontal bone that serves as the attachment site for the trochlea of the superior oblique muscle
A concave articular depression on the proximal ulna, formed by the olecranon and coronoid processes, that articulates with the trochlea of the humerus to form the hinge joint of the elbow
Ribs 1–7 that articulate directly with the sternum via individual costal cartilages, distinguishing them from false and floating ribs
concepts.shape: Curved, elongated bony arches with convex external and concave internal surfaces, flattened in cross-section, tapering from posterior to anterior, directly articulating with the sternum via costal cartilage
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, red bone marrow

concepts.description: Bony prominence on the lateral tibial condyle marking the distal attachment of the iliotibial tract, serving as a key landmark in knee biomechanics and IT band syndrome assessment
Small posterior projection on the neck of a rib that articulates with the transverse process of the corresponding thoracic vertebra, serving as a costovertebral joint attachment point
Bony projection on the palmar surface of the scaphoid's distal pole, serving as an attachment point for the flexor retinaculum and a palpable landmark at the wrist's radial side
A bony prominence on the palmar surface of the trapezium that forms the lateral border of the carpal tunnel and serves as an attachment site for the flexor retinaculum
Small bony prominence on the superior surface of the sphenoid bone, forming the anterior boundary of the sella turcica between the chiasmatic sulcus and the hypophyseal fossa
Roughened bony elevation on the inferior surface of the clavicle serving as the attachment site for the coracoclavicular ligament, stabilizing the acromioclavicular joint
Bony prominence on the plantar surface of the cuboid that serves as an attachment site for the long plantar ligament and provides a groove for the peroneus longus tendon

concepts.canonicalName: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of foot
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of foot
concepts.description: Roughened bony prominence on the plantar surface of each distal pedal phalanx that provides attachment for the fibrous digital pulp and supports soft tissue during weight-bearing
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of foot

concepts.canonicalName: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of hand
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of hand
concepts.description: Roughened bony projection on the palmar aspect of each distal phalanx of the hand that supports the fingertip pulp and serves as attachment for the fibrous septa of the digital pad
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Tuberosity of distal phalanx of hand

concepts.description: Bony projection on the medial surface of the navicular that serves as the primary attachment site for the tibialis posterior tendon
Roughened bony prominence on the anterior surface of the ulna, just distal to the coronoid process, serving as the insertion site for the brachialis muscle
Curved bony plate of the temporal bone that forms the anterior wall, floor, and part of the posterior wall of the external acoustic meatus
Concave articular facet on the medial aspect of the distal radius that articulates with the head of the ulna, forming the distal radioulnar joint
Bony projection at the distal medial aspect of the ulna that serves as an attachment site for the ulnar collateral ligament and the articular disc of the wrist
Bony projection arising from the superolateral margins of cervical vertebral bodies C3–C7, forming the lateral borders of the intervertebral disc and the uncovertebral joints of Luschka

concepts.canonicalName: Uncinate process of first thoracic vertebra
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Uncinate process of first thoracic vertebra
concepts.description: Bony hook-like projection extending superolaterally from the posterolateral lip of the T1 vertebral body, forming the medial border of the uncovertebral joint
Bony projection of the sphenoid's medial pterygoid plate that articulates with the sphenoidal rostrum and vomer, forming part of the roof of the nasal cavity
Bony segment of the axial skeleton forming the vertebral column, consisting of a body, vertebral arch, and processes that protect the spinal cord and support body weight
concepts.shape: Irregular bony segment with a roughly cylindrical anterior body and a posterior arch enclosing a central foramen, presenting superior, inferior, anterior, posterior, and lateral surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage

arteries.text: Spinal branch of posterior intercostal artery
Spinal branch of lateral sacral artery
Spinal branch of deep cervical artery
Spinal branch of ascending cervical artery

concepts.description: Bony ring projecting posteriorly from the vertebral body, formed by paired pedicles and laminae, enclosing the vertebral foramen and serving as attachment for processes

arteries.text: Spinal branches of posterior intercostal arteries
Spinal branches of lateral sacral arteries
Dorsal branches of posterior intercostal arteries

concepts.description: Cylindrical anterior portion of a vertebra composed of cancellous bone encased in cortical bone, providing primary axial load-bearing support for the spinal column
Opening within each vertebra formed between the vertebral body anteriorly and the vertebral arch posteriorly, collectively forming the vertebral canal that houses and protects the spinal cord
Tooth surface facing the oral vestibule, encompassing both labial and buccal aspects, oriented toward the lips and cheeks respectively
Facial skeleton comprising the 14 bones that form the orbit, nasal cavity, and jaws, derived embryologically from pharyngeal arch mesenchyme and distinct from the neurocranium
Anterior projection of the arytenoid cartilage that serves as the posterior attachment point for the vocal ligament, directly influencing vocal fold tension and phonation
Bony ridge on the greater wing of the sphenoid that articulates with the zygomatic bone, forming part of the lateral orbital wall boundary
Bony projection of the frontal bone that articulates with the frontal process of the zygomatic bone, forming the lateral orbital margin and contributing to the temporomandibular region

concepts.canonicalName: 1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral artery
concepts.displayNameEnglish: 1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral artery
sourceObjectNames: 1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral artery.l
1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral artery.r
concepts.description: Arterial branches arising sequentially from the deep femoral artery that perforate the adductor magnus to supply the posterior compartment muscles and femur

sourceObjectNames: 1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral vein.l
1th to 4th perforating branches of the deep femoral vein.r
concepts.description: Venous tributaries of the deep femoral vein that drain the posterior thigh compartment, corresponding to and accompanying the four perforating branches of the deep femoral artery
A superficial lower limb vein that drains the medial or posterior thigh and joins the great saphenous vein, serving as an anatomical variant with clinical relevance in venous insufficiency

concepts.canonicalName: Accompanying veins of arcuate and dorsal arteries
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Accompanying veins of arcuate and dorsal arteries
sourceObjectNames: Accompanying veins of arcuate and dorsal arteries.l
Accompanying veins of arcuate and dorsal arteries.r
concepts.description: Paired venae comitantes that travel alongside the arcuate and dorsal penile arteries, draining erectile tissue into the deep dorsal venous system

concepts.canonicalName: Accompanying veins of dorsal digital metatarsal arteries
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Accompanying veins of dorsal digital metatarsal arteries
sourceObjectNames: Accompanying veins of dorsal digital metatarsal arteries.l
Accompanying veins of dorsal digital metatarsal arteries.r
concepts.description: Paired venae comitantes that accompany the dorsal digital metatarsal arteries on the dorsum of the foot, draining the toes into the dorsal venous arch
Branch of the axillary artery that wraps anteriorly around the surgical neck of the humerus, supplying the glenohumeral joint and deltoid muscle
Superficial vein of the anterior thigh that drains cutaneous tissue and empties into the femoral or great saphenous vein
Branch of the common interosseous artery that descends along the anterior interosseous membrane of the forearm, supplying deep flexor muscles and the radius and ulna
Paired deep forearm veins accompanying the anterior interosseous artery between the radius and ulna, draining into the ulnar veins
A branch of the anterior tibial artery that arises proximal to the ankle and supplies the lateral malleolar network and adjacent joint structures
Branch of the anterior tibial artery supplying the medial malleolus, anastomosing with the posterior tibial and medial plantar arteries to form the medial malleolar network
Branch of the popliteal artery that passes through the interosseous membrane into the anterior compartment of the leg, supplying the dorsiflexor muscles and continuing as the dorsalis pedis artery

sourceObjectNames: Medial subtendinous bursa of gastrocnemius muscle.l

concepts.description: Synovial bursa located between the medial head of the gastrocnemius muscle and the posterior medial femoral condyle, reducing friction during knee flexion and extension
concepts.shape: Small, flattened ovoid synovial sac with smooth anterior and posterior surfaces, situated between the medial gastrocnemius tendon and the posterior femoral condyle
concepts.composition: dense irregular connective tissue, synovial membrane, synovial fluid

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular plexus of popliteal artery

landmarks.text: Medial head of gastrocnemius muscle origin
Posteromedial joint line of the knee

concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Medial subtendinous bursa of gastrocnemius muscle

concepts.description: Ligament connecting the medial tubercle of the talus to the sustentaculum tali, reinforcing the medial aspect of the subtalar joint
Strong fibrous bands uniting adjacent metatarsal bases internally, reinforcing the tarsometatarsal articulations and limiting splaying of the forefoot under load
Glenohumeral capsular ligament extending obliquely from the anterior glenoid rim to the lesser tubercle of the humerus, providing mid-range anterior shoulder stability
A fibrous band of the forearm extending from the lateral aspect of the ulnar tuberosity to just below the radial tuberosity, reinforcing the proximal radioulnar joint
Fibrous band of the first metacarpophalangeal joint that runs obliquely, providing rotational stability and resisting lateral deviation of the thumb
Fibrous sheet spanning the obturator foramen of the pelvis, leaving a superior canal for passage of the obturator vessels and nerve
concepts.shape: Thin, flat fibrous sheet of ovoid outline spanning the obturator foramen, presenting pelvic and obturator external surfaces with a slightly concave medial face
concepts.composition: dense fibrous connective tissue, fibrocartilage

landmarks.text: Superior margin along the obturator groove of the superior pubic ramus
Inferior margin along the ischiopubic ramus
Lateral attachment to the inner margin of the obturator foramen

concepts.description: Palmar intrinsic carpal ligament connecting the capitate to the hamate, stabilizing the fourth and fifth carpometacarpal joints and resisting dorsal displacement
Palmar ligaments reinforcing the carpometacarpal joints anteriorly, connecting carpal bones to metacarpal bases and stabilizing the hand's skeletal framework

concepts.canonicalName: Palmar ligaments of distal phalangeal joints
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Palmar ligaments of distal phalangeal joints
sourceObjectNames: Palmar ligaments of distal phalangeal joints.l
Palmar ligaments of distal phalangeal joints.r
concepts.description: Fibrocartilaginous ligaments on the palmar surfaces of the interphalangeal joints of the fingers, reinforcing the joint capsules and resisting hyperextension
Fibrocartilaginous ligaments on the volar aspect of the finger interphalangeal joints that resist hyperextension and reinforce the joint capsule
Dense fibrocartilaginous plates on the palmar aspect of MCP joints that reinforce the joint capsule and limit hyperextension
Intrinsic palmar wrist ligament connecting the lunate to the triquetrum along their volar aspect, stabilizing the proximal carpal row against rotational and shear forces
Transverse ligaments connecting the palmar surfaces of the second through fifth metacarpal bases, stabilizing the carpometacarpal joints and limiting metacarpal divergence
Palmar ligament of the distal radioulnar joint that reinforces the anterior capsule, spanning the radius to ulna, and resists supination while stabilizing the ulnar head
Thick, intrinsic wrist ligament extending from the anterior radius to the proximal carpal row, reinforcing the radiocarpal joint and resisting dorsal displacement
Palmar intrinsic carpal ligament connecting the scaphoid to the triquetrum on the volar aspect, contributing to proximal row stability and load transmission
Palmar intrinsic carpal ligament connecting the trapezoid to the capitate, stabilizing the distal carpal row against dorsopalmar and rotational forces
Intrinsic wrist ligament originating from the ulnar styloid and articular disc, coursing palmarly to attach to the lunate and triquetrum, stabilizing the ulnocarpal joint
A synovial bursa located at the medial proximal tibia, deep to the conjoined tendons of the sartorius, gracilis, and semitendinosus muscles, reducing friction during knee movement
concepts.shape: Thin, flat fluid-filled sac with an ovoid to crescent-shaped outline, positioned superficially beneath the conjoined tendons, presenting smooth superior and inferior surfaces separated by a minimal potential space
concepts.composition: synovial membrane, loose connective tissue, fibrous connective tissue, synovial fluid

arteries.text: Saphenous branch of descending genicular artery

landmarks.text: Medial joint line of the knee
Conjoined insertion of sartorius, gracilis, and semitendinosus tendons

concepts.description: Short ligament connecting the pisiform bone to the hook of the hamate, serving as a fibrous continuation of the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon within the wrist
Fibrous ligament extending from the pisiform bone to the bases of the fourth and fifth metacarpals, transmitting forces from the flexor carpi ulnaris tendon distally
Short intrinsic carpal ligament connecting the pisiform to the triquetrum, stabilizing the pisotriquetral joint and supporting ulnar-side wrist mechanics
Short plantar ligament connecting the plantar surfaces of the calcaneus and cuboid bones, resisting lateral longitudinal arch flattening and reinforcing the calcaneocuboid joint inferiorly
Strong fibrocartilaginous ligament extending from the sustentaculum tali to the navicular bone, supporting the head of the talus and maintaining the medial longitudinal arch
Plantar ligament connecting the cuboid and navicular bones, reinforcing the midtarsal joint and supporting the longitudinal arch of the foot
Plantar ligament connecting the lateral cuneiform to the cuboid bone, reinforcing the cuneocuboid joint inferiorly and supporting the lateral longitudinal arch
Plantar ligaments connecting the navicular bone to the three cuneiform bones, reinforcing the tarsometatarsal joint complex on the inferior foot surface
Short plantar ligaments connecting the adjacent surfaces of the cuneiform bones on their plantar aspects, reinforcing the transverse arch of the foot
Thick fibrous bands on the plantar surfaces of the metatarsophalangeal joints connecting adjacent metatarsal heads, reinforcing the deep transverse metatarsal ligament
Plantar ligaments reinforcing the tarsometatarsal joints inferiorly, connecting tarsal bones to metatarsal bases and resisting dorsiflexion forces across the midfoot

concepts.canonicalName: Plantar tendinous sheath of fibularis longus
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Plantar tendinous sheath of fibularis longus
sourceObjectNames: Plantar tendinous sheath of fibularis longus.l
Plantar tendinous sheath of fibularis longus.r
concepts.description: Synovial sheath encasing the fibularis longus tendon as it traverses the plantar surface of the foot toward its insertion on the medial cuneiform and first metatarsal base

concepts.composition: synovial membrane, dense irregular connective tissue, fibrous tissue

structure.text: Distal reflection at medial cuneiform and first metatarsal base

landmarks.text: Plantar groove of the cuboid bone
Long plantar ligament forming the fibro-osseous tunnel over the cuboid groove
Tuberosity of the cuboid bone at the lateral margin of the sheath

concepts.taPreferredLatin: Vagina plantaris tendinis musculi fibularis longi
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Plantar tendinous sheath of fibularis longus

concepts.description: Strong intracapsular knee ligament connecting the posterior tibial plateau to the medial femoral condyle, preventing posterior tibial displacement and resisting hyperflexion
Posterior fibrocartilaginous segment of the lateral meniscus anchored near the posterior intercondylar area, providing load distribution and stabilization of the lateral tibiofemoral compartment

nerves.text: Posterior articular branch of common fibular nerve
Recurrent articular branch of common fibular nerve

concepts.description: Posterior crescent-shaped fibrocartilaginous region of the medial meniscus anchored to the posterior intercondylar fossa, providing tibial plateau load distribution and rotational stability

nerves.text: Posterior articular branch of tibial nerve

concepts.description: Ligament of the knee joint extending from the posterior horn of the lateral meniscus to the medial femoral condyle, passing posterior to the posterior cruciate ligament
Fibrous ligament reinforcing the posterior aspect of the sternoclavicular joint, connecting the sternal end of the clavicle to the manubrium, resisting anterior displacement
Short ligament connecting the posterior tubercle of the talus to the upper calcaneus, stabilizing the subtalar joint against excessive posterior displacement
Strong fibrous band of the distal tibiofibular syndesmosis that connects the posterior tibial tubercle to the fibular malleolus, reinforcing ankle mortise stability
Strong ligamentous band of the medial (deltoid) ligament complex connecting the posterior tibial malleolus to the posterior talar body, resisting external rotation and eversion
Thickened band of the hip joint capsule extending from the pubic portion of the acetabular rim to the intertrochanteric fossa, resisting excessive abduction and external rotation
A short fibrous band connecting the distal radius to the ulnar neck, reinforcing the inferior radioulnar joint and limiting supination and pronation
A fibro-osseous ring encircling the radial head that holds it against the proximal radioulnar joint, allowing rotation while preventing lateral displacement
Lateral elbow ligament extending from the lateral epicondyle of the humerus to the annular ligament, stabilizing the radioulnar and humeroulnar joints against varus stress
Lateral wrist ligament connecting the radial styloid process to the scaphoid and trapezium, stabilizing the radiocarpal joint against ulnar deviation forces
Fan-shaped wrist ligament radiating from the capitate bone to surrounding carpal bones, providing multiplanar stability to the midcarpal joint
Extrinsic volar wrist ligament extending from the radial styloid to the scaphoid and capitate, providing critical carpal stability and acting as a scaphoid fulcrum
Pelvic ligament extending from the lateral sacrum and coccyx to the ischial spine, converting the greater sciatic notch into the greater sciatic foramen
Strong fibrous ligament spanning from the sacrum and coccyx to the ischial tuberosity, resisting nutation and forming the lesser sciatic foramen boundary
Intrinsic palmar wrist ligament connecting the scaphoid to the capitate, contributing to midcarpal stability and force transmission across the proximal and distal carpal rows
Intrinsic wrist ligament connecting the scaphoid and lunate bones, stabilizing the proximal carpal row and preventing dissociative instability when torn
Intrinsic carpal ligament connecting the scaphoid to both the trapezium and trapezoid, stabilizing the distal scaphoid articulation and resisting dorsal displacement

concepts.canonicalName: Sciatic bursa of gluteus maximus (Ischiogluteal bursa)

concepts.description: Synovial bursa located between the gluteus maximus muscle and the ischial tuberosity, reducing friction during sitting and hip movement

structure.text: Communication ostium with knee joint capsule (when present)
Deep recess abutting medial tibial condyle

nerves.text: Posterior articular branch of tibial nerve
