structure.text: Tendinous groove posterior to the talus
Plantar tunnel beneath the sustentaculum tali
Distal tendinous insertion on the base of the distal phalanx of the hallux

arteries.text: Muscular branches of posterior tibial artery

lymphatics.text: Deep lymphatic vessels of the leg

landmarks.text: Groove on the posterior aspect of the talus for the tendon
Sustentaculum tali of the calcaneus forming a fibro-osseous tunnel for the tendon
Posterior fibular surface serving as the proximal attachment site
Base of the distal phalanx of the great toe as the distal attachment landmark

concepts.description: A forearm muscle originating from the radius and interosseous membrane that flexes the interphalangeal joint of the thumb, being the only muscle capable of this action
concepts.shape: Long, slender fusiform muscle with a narrow proximal belly that tapers into a flattened tendinous band, occupying the deep anterior compartment of the forearm
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

structure.text: Radial origin from the anterior surface of the radius
Long tendon passing through the carpal tunnel
Terminal insertion on the base of the distal phalanx of the thumb

landmarks.text: Tendon palpable at the wrist radial to the flexor carpi radialis tendon
Tendon palpable within the thenar region as it courses to the thumb
Anterior surface of the radius serving as the primary bony origin landmark
Groove on the trapezium and first metacarpal guiding the distal tendon

concepts.description: A fibrous band spanning the medial ankle from the medial malleolus to the calcaneus, retaining flexor tendons, the posterior tibial artery, and tibial nerve within the tarsal tunnel
concepts.shape: A broad, flat fibrous band of dense connective tissue spanning the medial ankle, forming a shallow concave roof over the tarsal tunnel with smooth deep and superficial surfaces
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous connective tissue, periosteum (at bony attachments)

structure.text: Medial malleolar attachment — proximal anchor of the retinaculum to the tip and posterior border of the medial malleolus
Calcaneal attachment — distal anchor inserting into the medial surface of the calcaneus and adjoining plantar fascia
Osseofibrous tunnel for tibialis posterior tendon — most anterior and superior compartment transmitting the tibialis posterior tendon
Osseofibrous tunnel for flexor digitorum longus tendon — intermediate compartment transmitting the flexor digitorum longus tendon
Osseofibrous tunnel for posterior tibial vessels and tibial nerve — central neurovascular compartment
Osseofibrous tunnel for flexor hallucis longus tendon — most posterior and inferior compartment transmitting the flexor hallucis longus tendon
Fibrous septa — vertical partitions projecting from the deep surface that separate and define the individual osseofibrous tunnels

arteries.text: Medial malleolar branches of posterior tibial artery
Calcaneal branches of posterior tibial artery

lymphatics.text: Superficial lymphatic vessels of the medial ankle

nerves.text: Medial calcaneal branches of tibial nerve

landmarks.text: Medial malleolus — bony prominence marking the superoanterior attachment of the retinaculum, palpable subcutaneously
Sustentaculum tali — shelf-like calcaneal projection palpable approximately 2.5 cm below the medial malleolus, underlying the retinaculum
Posterior tibial pulse point — palpable pulsation of the posterior tibial artery located midway between the medial malleolus and the calcaneal tuberosity deep to the retinaculum
Calcaneal tuberosity — inferior bony landmark delineating the posteroinferior border of the retinaculum's calcaneal attachment

concepts.description: A fibrous band of deep fascia spanning the medial ankle or wrist that retains flexor tendons in position, forming the carpal or tarsal tunnel
concepts.shape: A flat, thick fibrous band of dense connective tissue forming a broad transverse roof over the carpal tunnel, with anterior and posterior surfaces and taut lateral margins
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous tissue, collagen fibers

structure.text: Medial attachment — anchors to the pisiform and hook of hamate on the ulnar side
Lateral attachment — anchors to the tubercle of scaphoid and tubercle of trapezium on the radial side
Carpal tunnel — the osseofibrous canal formed between the flexor retinaculum and the concave palmar arch of the carpal bones, transmitting the flexor tendons and median nerve
Ulnar canal (Guyon's canal) — a passage formed at the medial aspect between the flexor retinaculum and the palmar carpal ligament, transmitting the ulnar nerve and ulnar vessels
Superficial layer — the component through which the palmaris longus tendon inserts and that gives origin to thenar and hypothenar muscles
Deep layer — the primary restraining component forming the roof of the carpal tunnel proper

arteries.text: Palmar carpal branch of ulnar artery
Palmar carpal branch of radial artery

lymphatics.text: Superficial lymphatic vessels of the wrist

nerves.text: Palmar cutaneous branch of median nerve
Palmar cutaneous branch of ulnar nerve

landmarks.text: Pisiform — palpable medial bony landmark at the ulnar attachment of the retinaculum
Hook of hamate — palpable bony process forming the distal-medial anchor of the retinaculum
Tubercle of scaphoid — palpable radial-proximal bony landmark at the lateral attachment
Tubercle of trapezium — palpable radial-distal bony landmark at the lateral attachment
Palmaris longus tendon — superficial midline tendon serving as a surface guide to the midpoint of the retinaculum

concepts.description: Fibrous connective tissue sheet covering the gluteus medius muscle, serving as the origin for part of the tensor fasciae latae and blending with the iliotibial tract laterally
concepts.shape: Broad, flat fibrous sheet with smooth superficial and deep surfaces, covering the lateral gluteal region, tapering slightly at its margins into a thin, expansive quadrilateral form
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous aponeurosis

concepts.description: Largest muscle of the gluteal region, originating from the ilium, sacrum, and coccyx, inserting into the iliotibial tract and gluteal tuberosity, primarily responsible for hip extension and lateral rotation
concepts.shape: Large, thick, quadrilateral-shaped muscle with broad superficial and deep surfaces, coarse oblique fiber orientation, and prominent convex posterior contour forming the bulk of the buttock
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense irregular connective tissue, fascia

landmarks.text: Posterior superior iliac spine (proximal reference point of origin)
Gluteal tuberosity of femur (distal insertion palpation point)

concepts.description: A fan-shaped gluteal muscle originating from the ilium and inserting onto the greater trochanter, primarily abducting the thigh and stabilizing the pelvis during gait
concepts.shape: Fan-shaped, thick muscle with a broad superior base, tapering to a rounded inferior apex, featuring convex lateral and concave medial surfaces
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

arteries.text: Deep branch of superior gluteal artery
Superficial branch of superior gluteal artery

landmarks.text: Anterior superior iliac spine (anterior orientation landmark)
Posterior superior iliac spine (posterior orientation landmark)

concepts.description: Smallest of the three gluteal muscles, originating from the ilium and inserting on the greater trochanter, functioning to abduct and medially rotate the thigh
concepts.shape: Fan-shaped, thin, flat muscle with a broad superior origin tapering to a narrow inferior tendinous insertion, presenting anterior and posterior surfaces with a smooth convex outer face
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

structure.text: Origin on the outer surface of the ilium
Trochanteric bursa (subtendinous bursa of gluteus minimus)

arteries.text: Deep branch of superior gluteal artery

landmarks.text: Anterior gluteal line of the ilium (superior boundary of origin)
Inferior gluteal line of the ilium (inferior boundary of origin)
Greater trochanter of the femur (site of tendinous insertion on anterior facet)
Greater sciatic notch (inferior relation used for orientation)

concepts.description: A long, thin superficial muscle of the medial thigh originating from the pubic symphysis and inserting into the medial tibia, functioning in hip adduction and knee flexion
concepts.shape: A long, strap-like, flat muscle forming the most medial structure of the thigh, narrow superiorly where it arises from the pubis and slightly wider inferiorly as it tapers into a slender rounded tendon near the knee
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

landmarks.text: Pubic body and inferior pubic ramus attachment point
Pes anserinus on the medial tibial surface
Medial thigh furrow demarcating gracilis from adductor longus

concepts.canonicalName: Humeral head of extensor carpi ulnaris
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Humeral head of extensor carpi ulnaris
sourceObjectNames: Humeral head of extensor carpi ulnaris.l
Humeral head of extensor carpi ulnaris.r
concepts.description: Proximal attachment of extensor carpi ulnaris muscle arising from the lateral epicondyle of the humerus via the common extensor tendon
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Humeral head of extensor carpi ulnaris

concepts.canonicalName: Humeral head of flexor carpi ulnaris
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Humeral head of flexor carpi ulnaris
sourceObjectNames: Humeral head of flexor carpi ulnaris.l
Humeral head of flexor carpi ulnaris.r
concepts.description: Proximal attachment of flexor carpi ulnaris muscle arising from the medial epicondyle of the humerus via the common flexor tendon
Proximal head of pronator teres muscle arising from the medial epicondyle of the humerus and common flexor tendon, contributing to forearm pronation
Triangular hip flexor muscle originating from the iliac fossa and joining the psoas tendon to insert on the lesser trochanter of the femur
concepts.shape: A broad, fan-shaped, flat triangular muscle occupying the iliac fossa, with a wide superior origin that converges inferiorly into a tendinous attachment, presenting anterior and posterior surfaces and medial and lateral borders
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, fascia

structure.text: Iliac head (main belly originating from iliac fossa)
Accessory head (small slip arising from anterior inferior iliac spine or anterior capsule of hip joint)
Muscular belly (fleshy, fan-shaped mass within iliac fossa)
Iliopsoas tendon (conjoint tendon formed by fusion with psoas major tendon)
Iliac fascia (investing fascial layer covering the anterior surface of the muscle)

landmarks.text: Iliac fossa (concave origin surface of the ilium)
Anterior inferior iliac spine (partial origin attachment point)
Lesser trochanter of femur (distal insertion point via iliopsoas tendon)
Inguinal ligament (superficial relation marking transition from pelvis to thigh)

concepts.description: A dense longitudinal band of fascia lata extending from the iliac crest to the lateral tibial condyle, stabilizing the knee and hip during locomotion
concepts.shape: A broad, flat, longitudinal band of dense fibrous tissue extending along the lateral thigh, with a thick anterior surface, smooth posterior surface, and tapering inferior margin
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous connective tissue

structure.text: Proximal iliac portion, arising from the iliac crest and incorporating the fascia lata
Tensor fasciae latae insertion, receiving the tendinous fibers of the tensor fasciae latae muscle into the anterior aspect
Gluteal sling, receiving the tendinous fibers of the gluteus maximus muscle into the posterior aspect
Mid-thigh band, the taut intermediate segment overlying the lateral femoral condyle
Distal tibial insertion, attaching to Gerdy's tubercle on the anterolateral aspect of the lateral tibial condyle
Lateral intermuscular septum, a deep fibrous septum projecting medially to attach to the linea aspera of the femur

landmarks.text: Gerdy's tubercle, the bony prominence on the anterolateral tibial condyle marking the distal attachment
Iliac tubercle, the prominence on the iliac crest near the proximal origin
Lateral femoral epicondyle, the bony landmark over which the tract passes and where impingement commonly occurs
Lateral femoral condyle, palpable beneath the tract at approximately 30 degrees of knee flexion

concepts.description: Y-shaped fibrous band at the ankle securing extensor tendons across the dorsum of the foot, with stem attaching to the calcaneus and limbs anchoring to the medial malleolus and plantar fascia
concepts.shape: Y-shaped fibrous band spanning the anterior ankle, with a broader lateral stem and two diverging medial limbs creating an oblique cruciate pattern across the dorsal foot
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous connective tissue

structure.text: Inferior limb (attaches to medial cuneiform and plantar aponeurosis)
Intermediate septum (divides compartments for extensor digitorum longus and peroneus tertius)
Lateral compartment (contains extensor digitorum longus and peroneus tertius tendons)
Deep surface synovial-lined tunnels (tendon sheaths investing individual extensor tendons)

landmarks.text: Lateral calcaneal attachment site on the anterosuperior surface of the calcaneus
Medial malleolus attachment of the superior limb
Medial cuneiform attachment of the inferior limb
Palpable leading edge crossing the dorsum of the foot proximal to the tarsometatarsal joints

concepts.description: A fibrous band extending from the lateral calcaneus that retains the peroneus longus and brevis tendons inferior to the lateral malleolus, preventing bowstringing during foot eversion
concepts.shape: Thin, oblique fibrous band of moderate width stretching horizontally across the lateral ankle, presenting flat superior and inferior surfaces with tapered lateral margins
concepts.composition: dense regular connective tissue, fibrous connective tissue

concepts.description: A paired deep gluteal muscle originating from the ischial tuberosity that inserts into the obturator internus tendon, assisting in lateral rotation of the extended hip
concepts.shape: Small, flat, triangular muscle with a broad medial base tapering to a tendinous lateral apex, presenting anterior and posterior surfaces separated by superior and inferior borders
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue

landmarks.text: Greater trochanter of femur (site of insertion via obturator internus tendon)
Inferior border of obturator internus tendon (superior boundary of muscle)

concepts.description: Adipose tissue mass located beneath the patellar tendon within the anterior knee joint, cushioning the joint from mechanical stress and facilitating synovial fluid distribution
concepts.shape: Triangular wedge-shaped adipose mass occupying the anterior infrapatellar space, with a broad superior surface, tapered inferior apex, and flattened posterior surface facing the joint cavity
concepts.composition: adipose tissue, dense irregular connective tissue, synovial membrane, fibrous septa, blood vessels, sensory nerve endings

structure.text: Fibrous septa dividing lobules of adipose tissue

nerves.text: Articular branches of common fibular nerve
Sympathetic perivascular plexus of genicular arteries

landmarks.text: Inferior pole of patella — forms the superior border of the fat pad
Tibial tuberosity — marks the inferior attachment boundary of the overlying patellar tendon adjacent to the fat pad
Medial and lateral soft-tissue dimples flanking the patellar tendon — palpable surface recesses overlying the alar folds
Patellar tendon — anterior boundary palpable overlying the fat pad

concepts.description: Rotator cuff muscle originating from the infraspinous fossa of the scapula that inserts onto the greater tubercle of the humerus, primarily responsible for external rotation of the arm
concepts.shape: Broad, triangular, flat muscle occupying the infraspinous fossa of the scapula, with a wide posterior surface tapering to a rounded tendinous lateral apex
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue, perimysium, epimysium

landmarks.text: Medial border of the scapula (medial attachment)
Greater tubercle of the humerus (tendinous insertion)

concepts.description: A fibrous partition of the fascia lata extending inward to the linea aspera, separating the anterior quadriceps compartment from the posterior hamstring musculature of the thigh

arteries.text: Perforating branches of deep femoral artery

veins.text: Perforating veins of deep femoral vein

concepts.canonicalName: Lateral head of flexor hallucis brevis
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Lateral head of flexor hallucis brevis
sourceObjectNames: Lateral head of flexor hallucis brevis.l
Lateral head of flexor hallucis brevis.r
concepts.description: Medial compartment foot muscle head originating from the cuboid and lateral cuneiform, inserting into the lateral aspect of the proximal phalanx of the great toe via a sesamoid bone
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Lateral head of flexor hallucis brevis

concepts.description: Superficial calf muscle head originating from the lateral femoral condyle, forming the lateral bulk of the gastrocnemius, which plantarflexes the foot and flexes the knee
Muscle head originating from the posterior humerus above the radial groove, forming the lateral bulk of the triceps brachii and contributing to forearm extension at the elbow

Broad, flat skeletal muscle of the posterior trunk originating from the thoracolumbar fascia and iliac crest, inserting into the intertubercular groove of the humerus to perform adduction, extension, and medial rotation of the arm
concepts.shape: Broad, flat, triangular muscle forming the posterior axillary fold, with a wide costal origin tapering to a narrow tendinous insertion, covering much of the lower back
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

landmarks.text: Inferior angle of the scapula (crossed by muscle belly)
Spinous processes of lower thoracic and lumbar vertebrae (along origin)

concepts.description: A posterior neck muscle originating from the transverse processes of C1–C4 vertebrae and inserting onto the superior angle of the scapula, functioning to elevate and downwardly rotate the scapula
concepts.shape: Elongated, strap-like muscle with a narrow superior origin that broadens inferiorly into a flat, ribbon-shaped belly tapering toward its scapular insertion
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue, epimysium, perimysium, endomysium

structure.text: Superior muscular slips (four tendinous heads of origin from C1–C4 transverse processes)
Superior belly (upper cervical portion of the muscle belly)
Inferior belly (lower cervical and upper thoracic portion converging toward scapula)
Muscular belly (main fleshy central mass of the muscle)
Distal tendinous insertion (flattened tendinous expansion attaching to the superior angle and medial border of the scapula)

landmarks.text: Posterior tubercles of transverse processes of C1–C4 (proximal attachment points)
Superior angle of the scapula (primary distal bony landmark at insertion)
Medial border of the scapula superior to the spine (distal insertion ridge)
Posterior triangle of the neck (region where the muscle forms part of the floor and is palpable)

concepts.canonicalName: Long head of biceps brachii tendon sheath
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Long head of biceps brachii tendon sheath
sourceObjectNames: Long head of biceps brachii tendon sheath.l
Long head of biceps brachii tendon sheath.r
concepts.description: Synovial sheath enveloping the long head of biceps brachii tendon within the intertubercular groove, facilitating frictionless gliding and continuous with the glenohumeral joint capsule
Proximal head of the biceps brachii originating from the supraglenoid tubercle of the scapula, passing through the glenohumeral joint capsule within the intertubercular sulcus
Posterior thigh muscle originating from the ischial tuberosity, forming the long component of biceps femoris, which extends the hip and flexes the knee

arteries.text: Perforating branches of deep femoral artery

veins.text: Perforating veins of deep femoral vein

concepts.description: Muscle head originating from the infraglenoid tubercle of the scapula, forming the medial portion of the triceps brachii and contributing to both elbow extension and shoulder adduction
Four intrinsic foot muscles arising from flexor digitorum longus tendons that flex the metatarsophalangeal joints and extend the interphalangeal joints of the lateral four toes
concepts.shape: Four small, worm-shaped (vermiform) intrinsic muscles of the foot, each slender and fusiform, arising from the medial and adjacent sides of the flexor digitorum longus tendons and tapering distally toward the medial sides of the lesser toes.
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

structure.text: Origin from flexor digitorum longus tendons
Insertion into extensor expansion (dorsal digital expansion) of toes 2–5

nerves.text: Deep branch of lateral plantar nerve

landmarks.text: Medial aspect of flexor digitorum longus tendon at origin point
Medial side of proximal phalanx of each lesser toe at insertion

concepts.description: A fibrous partition of the thigh extending from the deep fascia lata inward to the femur, separating the medial muscular compartment from the anterior compartment

concepts.canonicalName: Medial head of flexor hallucis brevis
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Medial head of flexor hallucis brevis
sourceObjectNames: Medial head of flexor hallucis brevis.l
Medial head of flexor hallucis brevis.r
concepts.description: Medial muscle belly of flexor hallucis brevis that inserts into the medial aspect of the proximal phalanx of the great toe, enclosing a sesamoid bone
concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Medial head of flexor hallucis brevis

concepts.description: Medial muscle head of the gastrocnemius arising from the posterior medial femoral condyle, forming the larger, more prominent belly of the two-headed calf muscle
Deep muscle head of the triceps brachii originating from the posterior humerus below the radial groove, contributing to forearm extension at the elbow
A muscle head arising from the bases of metatarsals 2–4 and the fibular sheath, converging obliquely to insert at the lateral base of the proximal phalanx of the great toe

nerves.text: Deep branch of lateral plantar nerve

concepts.description: Deep muscle head arising from capitate bone and bases of second and third metacarpals, converging obliquely to insert at the proximal phalanx of the thumb to adduct it
A deep medial thigh muscle originating from the obturator membrane and surrounding bone, inserting into the trochanteric fossa, functioning as a lateral rotator of the hip joint
concepts.shape: Flat, fan-shaped muscle with a broad anterior surface and tapered posterior apex, exhibiting a triangular profile that widens medially across the obturator membrane
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue

concepts.description: A deep hip muscle originating from the inner surface of the obturator membrane and pelvis, passing through the lesser sciatic foramen to insert on the greater trochanter, externally rotating the extended thigh
concepts.shape: Fan-shaped flat muscle with a broad triangular pelvic surface and a narrower tendinous posterior surface that converges toward a rounded tendinous outlet
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, fascia

landmarks.text: Lesser sciatic notch (site of tendinous reflection)
Lesser sciatic foramen (exit point of tendon)

concepts.canonicalName: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of foot
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of foot
sourceObjectNames: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of foot.l
Opponens digiti minimi muscle of foot.r
concepts.description: Intrinsic foot muscle arising from the long plantar ligament and fibular sheath, inserting along the fifth metatarsal to assist opposition of the little toe
concepts.shape: Slender, elongated fusiform muscle with a flattened band-like form, narrow at its proximal origin and slightly wider distally, lying along the lateral plantar border of the foot
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, epimysium, perimysium, endomysium

structure.text: Proximal origin head, arising from the plantar surface of the long plantar ligament and the sheath of the fibularis longus tendon
Muscle belly, comprising the main contractile mass running obliquely along the lateral foot
Distal insertion tendon, attaching along the lateral aspect of the entire length of the fifth metatarsal shaft
Deep fascial layer, forming the floor of the lateral plantar compartment over the muscle
Neurovascular hilum, where the superficial branch of the lateral plantar nerve and vessels enter the muscle belly

nerves.text: Superficial branch of lateral plantar nerve

landmarks.text: Lateral border of the fifth metatarsal shaft, marking the distal insertion site
Tuberosity of the fifth metatarsal, serving as a proximal reference point near the muscle's origin
Long plantar ligament groove, indicating the proximal attachment region on the plantar foot

concepts.canonicalName: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of hand
concepts.displayNameEnglish: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of hand
sourceObjectNames: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of hand.l
Opponens digiti minimi muscle of hand.r
concepts.description: Intrinsic hand muscle of the hypothenar eminence that flexes and rotates the fifth metacarpal, enabling opposition of the little finger toward the thumb
concepts.shape: A small, flat, triangular muscle lying deep to the flexor digiti minimi brevis along the medial border of the hand, with a broad proximal attachment tapering to a narrow distal insertion along the fifth metacarpal shaft.
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue

structure.text: Proximal attachment head, arising from the hook of the hamate and the flexor retinaculum
Muscle belly, forming the deep layer of the hypothenar muscular mass
Distal insertion, attaching along the entire length of the medial border of the fifth metacarpal
Fascial sheath, enclosing the muscle within the hypothenar compartment

arteries.text: Deep palmar branch of ulnar artery

lymphatics.text: Deep lymphatic vessels of the palm

landmarks.text: Hook of the hamate, serving as the primary proximal bony anchor
Medial border of the fifth metacarpal shaft, defining the line of distal attachment
Flexor retinaculum, from which part of the proximal tendon originates

concepts.taPreferredEnglish: Opponens digiti minimi muscle of hand

concepts.description: Intrinsic thenar muscle originating from the flexor retinaculum and trapezium, inserting along the first metacarpal to medially rotate and oppose the thumb
concepts.shape: A small, flat, triangular muscle located deep to the abductor pollicis brevis on the radial aspect of the thenar eminence, with a broad proximal base and tapering apex, oriented obliquely across the anterior surface of the first metacarpal
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, fibrous connective tissue

structure.text: Proximal origin head arising from the flexor retinaculum and tubercle of the trapezium
Muscle belly forming the deep layer of the thenar eminence
Distal insertion along the radial border and shaft of the first metacarpal
Thenar fascial envelope investing the muscle belly

arteries.text: Superficial palmar branch of radial artery

landmarks.text: Tubercle of the trapezium serving as the proximal attachment point
Flexor retinaculum at the wrist as the proximal attachment site
Radial border of the first metacarpal as the distal insertion landmark
Thenar eminence bulge overlying the muscle on the palm

concepts.description: Small intrinsic muscle of the hand arising from the palmar aponeurosis and inserting into the skin of the hypothenar eminence, improving grip by wrinkling palmar skin
concepts.shape: A thin, quadrilateral cutaneous muscle located in the superficial palm, lying entirely within the subcutaneous tissue of the hypothenar eminence with a flat anterior surface facing the skin and a posterior surface resting on the palmar aponeurosis.
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle

structure.text: Medial origin from the pisiform bone and flexor retinaculum
Lateral insertion into the dermis of the medial border of the hand
Transversely oriented muscle fascicles spanning the hypothenar eminence
Superficial fibrous layer blending with overlying skin

arteries.text: Superficial palmar branch of ulnar artery

lymphatics.text: Superficial lymphatic vessels of the palm

landmarks.text: Pisiform bone at the medial origin point
Hypothenar skin crease visible upon contraction of the muscle
Medial border of the palmar aponeurosis underlying the muscle

concepts.description: A slender, superficial forearm muscle arising from the medial epicondyle and inserting into the palmar aponeurosis, frequently absent and commonly harvested for tendon grafting
concepts.shape: Slender, fusiform muscle with a short proximal belly and an exceptionally long, flat tendon, narrow in cross-section, tapering distally toward the palmar aponeurosis
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

landmarks.text: Visible distal tendon at the wrist when the thumb and little finger are opposed with the wrist flexed
Medial epicondyle of the humerus at the proximal attachment
Flexor retinaculum at the wrist, over which the tendon passes superficially
Palmar aponeurosis at the distal insertion point

concepts.description: A flat, quadrangular hip adductor muscle originating from the pectineal line of the pubis and inserting onto the pectineal line of the femur, acting to adduct and flex the thigh
concepts.shape: Flat, quadrangular muscle with a broad superior border, tapering inferolaterally into a short, narrow belly with anterior and posterior surfaces oriented in the femoral triangle
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, epimysium, perimysium, endomysium

structure.text: Proximal attachment (origin) at the pectineal line and pecten pubis of the pubic bone
Distal attachment (insertion) along the pectineal line of the femur between the lesser trochanter and linea aspera
Muscle belly comprising the bulk of parallel skeletal muscle fibers
Proximal tendon connecting the muscle to its pelvic origin
Distal tendon connecting the muscle belly to the femoral insertion
Fascial sheath derived from the iliopectineal fascia investing the muscle

landmarks.text: Pectineal line of the pubis (pecten pubis), the bony origin readily identified during surgical dissection
Pectineal line of the femur, the linear bony ridge marking the distal insertion
Femoral triangle boundary, where the medial border of the pectineus forms the medial wall
Lesser trochanter of the femur, a proximal landmark adjacent to the superior extent of insertion

concepts.description: Large, fan-shaped skeletal muscle of the anterior chest wall originating from the clavicle, sternum, and costal cartilages, inserting into the humerus to adduct, flex, and medially rotate the arm
concepts.shape: Large, fan-shaped muscle with a broad, flat anterior surface and a thick, rounded lateral border that converges into a narrow tendinous fold
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense regular connective tissue

arteries.text: Perforating branches of internal thoracic artery

landmarks.text: Crest of the greater tubercle of the humerus (insertion ridge)
Sternocostal origin along the sternum and costal cartilages
Infraclavicular fossa overlying the clavicular head

concepts.description: A small, flat triangular chest muscle arising from ribs 3–5 and inserting onto the coracoid process, functioning to stabilize, depress, and protract the scapula
concepts.shape: A flat, thin, triangular muscle located in the anterior thoracic wall, deep to the pectoralis major, with a broad inferior base arising from the ribs and a narrow apex converging superolaterally toward the coracoid process
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue, fascia

concepts.description: Conjoined tendinous insertion of the sartorius, gracilis, and semitendinosus muscles attaching to the anteromedial proximal tibia, forming a fan-shaped structure resembling a goose's foot

arteries.text: Saphenous branch of descending genicular artery

veins.text: Saphenous branch of descending genicular vein

concepts.description: Pear-shaped gluteal muscle originating from the anterior sacrum and inserting on the greater trochanter, functioning in lateral rotation and abduction of the thigh at the hip joint
concepts.shape: A flat, somewhat triangular muscle with a broad base arising from the anterior sacrum and a narrow apex that tapers into a rounded tendon, oriented obliquely within the greater sciatic foramen as it passes from the pelvis to the posterior hip.
concepts.composition: skeletal muscle, dense connective tissue

structure.text: Proximal (sacral) origin heads arising from the pelvic surface of the second through fourth sacral segments
Muscular belly forming the bulk of the triangular mass within the pelvis and greater sciatic foramen
Distal tendon condensing from the muscle fibers as they converge toward the greater trochanter
Insertion footprint on the superior border of the greater trochanter of the femur
Sacrotuberous ligament attachment contributing to the fascial origin at the pelvic floor

Articularis genus fiber group at the inferior margin

Distal convergence point at the superior pole of the patella via the quadriceps tendon

Muscle belly forming the main contractile mass with bipennate fiber arrangement
Distal aponeurosis converging toward the quadriceps tendon
Lateral intermuscular septum separating the muscle from the short head of the biceps femoris
Vastus lateralis obliquus, a distinct distal portion with more obliquely oriented fibers inserting into the lateral border of the patella
Quadriceps tendon contribution merging with tendons of other vasti and rectus femoris to attach to the patella

concepts.description: Medial component of the quadriceps femoris group originating from the intertrochanteric line and medial lip of linea aspera, inserting into the patella, and primarily responsible for knee extension with a stabilizing role on patellar tracking
concepts.shape: Elongated, teardrop-shaped muscle with a broad proximal belly tapering to a flat distal expansion, featuring a prominent medial convex surface and a deep posterior surface applied to the femur

Medial border of patella (distal tendinous insertion point)

concepts.description: Large paired bony structure forming the lateral pelvis, comprising fused ilium, ischium, and pubis, articulating with the sacrum posteriorly and femur laterally

concepts.description: Long bone of the upper arm articulating proximally with the glenoid fossa of the scapula and distally with the radius and ulna at the elbow joint
concepts.shape: Long tubular bone with a rounded proximal head, cylindrical shaft, and flattened distal end bearing anterior, posterior, medial, and lateral surfaces widening into a broad condylar region
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, articular cartilage, periosteum, endosteum, bone marrow

Arcuate artery of the humeral head

nerves.text: Periosteal branches of the radial nerve
Periosteal branches of the axillary nerve

concepts.description: A paired facial bone forming the lower lateral wall of the nasal cavity, projecting medially to increase mucosal surface area and turbulate airflow
concepts.shape: Thin, curved scroll-like bony plate with a medial concave surface and lateral convex surface, elongated horizontally within the nasal cavity, resembling a delicate curled shell
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum

concepts.description: The smallest of the three cuneiform bones, located in the distal tarsus between the medial and lateral cuneiforms, articulating with the second metatarsal distally
concepts.shape: Wedge-shaped tarsal bone, the smallest cuneiform, with a narrow plantar surface, broad dorsal surface, and flat medial, lateral, proximal, and distal articular facets
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, articular cartilage, periosteum

veins.text: Dorsal venous arch of the foot

concepts.description: Thin, paired facial bone forming the anteromedial orbital wall, containing the lacrimal groove that houses the nasolacrimal sac and duct
concepts.shape: Thin, delicate quadrilateral plate of compact bone with lateral and medial surfaces, forming part of the medial orbital wall, roughly fingernail-sized with smooth and ridged faces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum

landmarks.text: Posterior lacrimal crest — vertical ridge dividing the lateral surface into anterior fossa and posterior orbital plate
Lacrimal hamulus — hook-like inferior projection completing the superior opening of the nasolacrimal canal
Lacrimal groove (sulcus lacrimalis) — vertical furrow anterior to the posterior lacrimal crest lodging the lacrimal sac
Articular facets at borders — serrated margins for articulation with frontal, ethmoid, maxilla, and inferior nasal concha

concepts.description: Wedge-shaped tarsal bone located between the intermediate cuneiform medially and the cuboid laterally, articulating with the third metatarsal distally
concepts.shape: Wedge-shaped tarsal bone, smallest of the three cuneiforms, with a narrow plantar surface, broad dorsal surface, and flat medial, lateral, proximal, and distal articular facets
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, articular cartilage, periosteum

veins.text: Dorsal venous arch of the foot

concepts.description: A mandibular tooth positioned at the corner of the dental arch, characterized by a single cusp and root, functioning in tearing food and guiding occlusion
concepts.shape: Narrow, elongated tooth with a single pointed cusp, labial and lingual surfaces, mesial and distal contact facets, and a compressed root longer than adjacent incisors
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

landmarks.text: Cusp tip — the single pointed apex of the crown used as an occlusal reference point
Cervical line — the curved cemento-enamel junction demarcating crown from root
Labial ridge — a subtle vertical elevation running cervico-incisally along the mid-labial surface
Apical foramen — the opening at the root apex transmitting the neurovascular bundle

concepts.description: Permanent mandibular molar positioned sixth from the midline, typically the first permanent tooth to erupt around age six, bearing four or five cusps for grinding food
concepts.shape: Broad, multi-cusped crown with a rectangular occlusal outline, five cusps, four distinct surfaces—buccal, lingual, mesial, distal—and two robust divergent roots
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

arteries.text: Dental branches of inferior alveolar artery
Interalveolar branches of inferior alveolar artery
Perforating branches of inferior alveolar artery

nerves.text: Dental branches of inferior alveolar nerve
Sympathetic plexus of inferior alveolar artery

concepts.description: A mandibular premolar that is the first bicuspid distal to the canine, characterized by a dominant buccal cusp and a small, nonfunctional lingual cusp
concepts.shape: Small bicuspid tooth with a prominent buccal cusp, a diminutive lingual cusp, a compressed mesiodistal width, and a roughly trapezoidal occlusal surface with buccal, lingual, mesial, and distal faces
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

nerves.text: Sympathetic perivascular plexus of the inferior alveolar artery

concepts.description: A permanent mandibular incisor positioned lateral to the central incisor, slightly larger, with a more asymmetric crown used for cutting food
concepts.shape: Small, blade-like tooth with a narrow mesiodistal width, flattened labial and lingual surfaces, and a nearly symmetrical incisal edge with slightly convex proximal surfaces
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

nerves.text: Dental plexus of inferior alveolar nerve

concepts.description: A mandibular incisor positioned at the midline of the lower dental arch, characterized by its narrow mesiodistal width and role in anterior guidance and incisal function
concepts.shape: Small, chisel-shaped tooth with a narrow mesiodistal width, flat labial and lingual surfaces, and a straight incisal edge, forming a slender, blade-like crown with a single tapered root
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

concepts.description: Permanent molar tooth in the mandibular arch, positioned distal to the first molar and mesial to the third molar, bearing four cusps and two roots used in mastication
concepts.shape: Broad rectangular crown with four cusps, flattened mesial and distal surfaces, convex buccal and lingual surfaces, and a wide occlusal table, set on bifurcated roots
concepts.composition: enamel, dentine, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

Perforating branches of inferior alveolar artery

nerves.text: Dental branches of inferior alveolar nerve
Interalveolar branches of inferior alveolar nerve
Sympathetic plexus on inferior alveolar artery

concepts.description: A mandibular premolar positioned distal to the first premolar, featuring variable cusp morphology (two or three cusps) and serving as a transitional tooth between canines and molars
concepts.shape: Bicuspid tooth with a broad occlusal surface, two cusps of nearly equal height, convex buccal and lingual faces, and a roughly rectangular mesiodistal crown outline
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

structure.text: Lingual cusp (single or divided into mesiolingual and distolingual cusps in three-cusp variant)

landmarks.text: Central fossa on the occlusal surface
Buccal ridge running vertically on the buccal surface of the crown
Apical foramen at the root tip

concepts.description: A crescent-shaped carpal bone of the proximal row, articulating with the radius and ulnar notch proximally, prone to avascular necrosis following dislocation

Lateral facet ridge bordering the scaphoid articulation

concepts.shape: Horseshoe-shaped unpaired bone forming the lower jaw, with a curved horizontal body and two vertical rami angled posterosuperiorly, presenting external and internal surfaces

concepts.description: Uppermost and widest segment of the sternum, articulating with the clavicles and first two pairs of ribs, forming the sternal angle at its junction with the body

concepts.description: Largest of the three cuneiform bones, located on the medial side of the foot, articulating with the first metatarsal distally and the navicular proximally

Inferior articular facet of the head

concepts.description: A permanent tooth in the maxillary arch, positioned lateral to the incisors, characterized by a single cusp and long root used for tearing food
concepts.shape: Cone-shaped tooth with a single pointed cusp, convex labial surface, slightly concave lingual surface, and a long tapered root, larger and more robust than lower canines

concepts.description: Permanent maxillary tooth, the first molar erupting around age 6, characterized by four cusps, three roots, and the largest crown surface in the upper dental arch
concepts.shape: Broad, roughly cuboidal crown with four cusps, wide occlusal table, convex buccal and lingual surfaces, mesial and distal contact surfaces, and three divergent roots
concepts.composition: enamel, dentine, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

concepts.description: A maxillary premolar tooth, typically featuring two cusps and one or two roots, located between the canine and second premolar in the permanent dentition
concepts.shape: Bicuspid tooth with a broader buccal surface, flattened mesial and distal surfaces, a concave lingual surface, and an occlusal surface bearing two prominent cusps
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

Cervical line (cementoenamel junction demarcating crown from root)

concepts.description: A permanent maxillary tooth positioned distal to the central incisor, characterized by a single root and a smaller, peg-shaped or conical crown used for cutting food
concepts.shape: Small, peg-shaped or conical permanent tooth with a narrow mesiodistal width, convex labial surface, slightly concave lingual surface, and tapered incisal edge
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

concepts.description: A maxillary central incisor tooth positioned at the midline of the upper dental arch, characterized by a single root and a broad, chisel-shaped crown used for cutting food
concepts.shape: Mesiodistally broad, chisel-shaped crown with flat labial, slightly concave lingual, and two proximal surfaces tapering to a straight incisal edge, set on a single conical root

concepts.description: Permanent maxillary molar positioned distal to the first molar, featuring four cusps and three roots, used for grinding food in the posterior upper dental arch

concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

concepts.description: A maxillary posterior tooth positioned distal to the first premolar, featuring two cusps and a single root, used for crushing and grinding food during mastication
concepts.shape: Bicuspid crown with broad occlusal surface, two cusps of near-equal height, convex buccal and lingual faces, mesial and distal contact surfaces, and a single tapering root
concepts.composition: enamel, dentin, cementum, dental pulp, periodontal ligament

Prominent long horizontal spinous process, palpable at the base of the neck
Superior vertebral notch forming the roof of the intervertebral foramen

concepts.description: A thoracic vertebra located at the tenth level of the spinal column, typically the first to articulate with a single costal facet, marking the transition zone near the thoracolumbar region
concepts.shape: Roughly heart-shaped vertebral body with a complete costal facet, broad laminae, and a posteriorly directed spinous process, transitioning between thoracic and lower spinal morphology
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage

concepts.description: A thoracic vertebra located at the 11th level of the thoracic spine, characterized by a single costal facet on each pedicle and absence of transverse process facets
concepts.shape: Irregular bony ring with a broad, heart-shaped vertebral body, short robust pedicles, wide vertebral foramen, and nearly vertical, flattened spinous process with reduced articular facets
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, red bone marrow

concepts.description: The most inferior thoracic vertebra, transitional in morphology with features of both thoracic and lumbar vertebrae, bearing the last pair of floating ribs
concepts.shape: Wedge-shaped vertebral body with a thick, broad profile transitioning from thoracic to lumbar form, featuring flat superior and inferior surfaces, short robust transverse processes, and a wide vertebral foramen
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage

concepts.description: A thoracic vertebra distinguished by its articulation with the heads of ribs 2 and 3, long posteriorly angled spinous process, and transitional costal facet morphology between T1 and mid-thoracic levels
concepts.shape: Roughly heart-shaped vertebral body with a large, nearly circular vertebral foramen, long posteriorly-inclined spinous process, and broad, horizontally-oriented transverse surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, red bone marrow

concepts.description: A thoracic vertebra at the third level, distinguished by its articulation with the third pair of ribs and its position between T2 and T4 in the mid-upper thoracic spine
concepts.shape: Compact, heart-shaped vertebral body with a moderately large neural arch, broad flat spinous process angled inferiorly, and costal facets on the lateral body surfaces
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, red bone marrow

concepts.description: A thoracic vertebra at the 4th level, articulating with ribs 4 and 5, whose spinous process aligns with the sternal angle landmark at the T4–T5 intervertebral disc
concepts.shape: Roughly heart-shaped vertebral body with a cylindrical centrum, broad flat superior and inferior endplates, paired lateral costal facets, and a long posteriorly angled spinous process
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage, red bone marrow

concepts.description: The fifth thoracic vertebra, characterized by articulating facets for the fifth pair of ribs and positioned at the level of the sternal angle's inferior extent
concepts.shape: A mid-thoracic vertebra with a heart-shaped body, short pedicles, posteriorly angled spinous process, broad transverse processes, and triangular vertebral foramen
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum, articular cartilage, hyaline cartilage

concepts.shape: Wedge-shaped vertebral body with flat superior and inferior surfaces, a triangular vertebral foramen, and long posteriorly angled spinous process with robust transverse processes
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, hyaline cartilage, periosteum, red bone marrow

concepts.description: Unpaired flat cranial bone forming the posteroinferior nasal septum, articulating with the ethmoid, sphenoid, palatine bones, and maxillae
concepts.shape: Thin, plow-shaped quadrilateral bone with flat lateral surfaces, a superior grooved border, and an inferior free edge forming the posterior nasal septum
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum

landmarks.text: Alae of the vomer — wing-like projections at the superior border that articulate with the sphenoid rostrum
Vomerine groove — median groove along the superior border accommodating the septal cartilage anteriorly
Posterior free border — smooth, concave edge forming the posterior nasal septum, visible on posterior rhinoscopy
Choanal crest — the inferior border resting on the nasal crest of the maxilla and palatine bones

concepts.description: Paired facial bone forming the cheek prominence and lateral orbital wall, articulating with the frontal, temporal, maxillary, and sphenoid bones
concepts.shape: Irregular quadrilateral bone with lateral, orbital, temporal, and maxillary surfaces, roughly trapezoidal in profile, forming the prominent lateral cheek projection
concepts.composition: compact bone, cancellous bone, periosteum
